flow_map.v1_pipeline:
v1 lives under src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/ and exposes its full surface via v1/__init__.py:1-67. Stages are wired through DataJoint Manual / Computed / Lookup tables, each Selection row is UUID-keyed and inserted by an `insert_selection` classmethod, then a downstream Computed table is `populate()`-ed.

1) SortGroup (v1/recording.py:33-95) -- Manual + Part(SortGroupElectrode). User declares which Electrodes are sorted together. `set_group_by_shank` (line 50) inserts both the master and part rows; on rerun it silently deletes and re-inserts (the regression v2 explicitly fixes).
2) SpikeSortingPreprocessingParameters (v1/recording.py:98-143) -- Lookup with one default row (`"default"`, bandpass 300-6000Hz, seed=0).
3) SpikeSortingRecordingSelection (v1/recording.py:146-182) -- Manual, PK `recording_id` (uuid); FKs Raw, SortGroup, IntervalList, SpikeSortingPreprocessingParameters, LabTeam. Inserted via `insert_selection`.
4) SpikeSortingRecording (v1/recording.py:185-...) -- Computed; tri-part (`make_fetch`/`make_compute`/`make_insert`). Preprocesses recording, writes ElectricalSeries into a fresh AnalysisNwbfile, inserts the sort_interval valid_times into IntervalList under `interval_list_name = recording_id`. Also auto-registers a RecordingRecomputeSelection row (line 265-267).
5) ArtifactDetectionParameters (v1/artifact.py:29-93) -- Lookup; defaults `"default"` (3000uV) and `"none"`.
6) ArtifactDetectionSelection (v1/artifact.py:96-129) -- Manual, PK `artifact_id` (uuid); FK SpikeSortingRecording + ArtifactDetectionParameters. `insert_selection` (line 106).
7) ArtifactDetection (v1/artifact.py:132-207) -- Computed; reads back the recording NWB, calls `_get_artifact_times`, writes valid-times row to IntervalList under `interval_list_name = artifact_id`.
8) SpikeSorterParameters (v1/sorting.py:82-194) -- Lookup; defaults include mountainsort4 tetrode/cortex presets and the `clusterless_thresholder` row (with `noise_levels=np.asarray([1.0])` -- v1 baseline for the schema-defaults bug).
9) SpikeSortingSelection (v1/sorting.py:197-229) -- Manual, PK `sorting_id` (uuid); FKs SpikeSortingRecording, SpikeSorterParameters, IntervalList (the artifact interval, in practice).
10) SpikeSorting (v1/sorting.py:232-...) -- Computed; tri-part. Loads the recording, zeros artifact intervals via `sip.remove_artifacts`, runs the sorter, saves units to a new AnalysisNwbfile.
11) CurationV1 (v1/curation.py:29-...) -- Manual; PK adds `curation_id`. `insert_curation` (line 43) writes a curated NWB with labels/merge_groups/metrics. It is the SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV1 merge-table source (spikesorting_merge.py:105-110).
12) MetricCuration chain (v1/metric_curation.py:59-...): WaveformParameters + MetricParameters + MetricCurationParameters Lookups, MetricCurationSelection (FKs CurationV1 + the three Lookups), MetricCuration Computed (waveforms + quality metrics; calls `CurationV1.insert_metric_curation` to derive a child curation).
13) BurstPair chain (v1/burst_curation.py:40-...): BurstPairParams Lookup, BurstPairSelection FK MetricCuration + BurstPairParams, BurstPair Computed + part BurstPairUnit (xcorr/asymmetry per unit pair).
14) FigURLCuration chain (v1/figurl_curation.py:20-...): Selection FK CurationV1 with curation_uri + metrics_figurl, Computed generates a sortingview URL.
15) RecordingRecompute chain (v1/recompute.py:54-...): RecordingRecomputeVersions, RecordingRecomputeSelection, RecordingRecompute -- verifies AnalysisNwbfile reproducibility across SI versions.
flow_map.v2_pipeline:
v2 lives under src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/. v2/__init__.py:1-37 is intentionally minimal: the only eager symbol is `initialize_v2_defaults`, which `insert_default()`s PreprocessingParameters, ArtifactDetectionParameters, and SorterParameters. The orchestrator `run_v2_pipeline` (v2/pipeline.py:86-279) chains the canonical path.

1) SortGroupV2 (v2/recording.py:90-...) -- Manual + Part(SortGroupElectrode). `set_group_by_shank` enforces an inspect-before-destroy contract (preview_existing_entries at line 130; `_handle_existing` refuses to silently re-create rows on rerun -- the v1 regression). Optional `set_group_by_electrode_table_column` for arbitrary columns.
2) PreprocessingParameters (v2/recording.py:570-649) -- Lookup; row `params` blob is Pydantic-validated by PreprocessingParamsSchema, with explicit `params_schema_version=2`. Defaults: `default_franklab`, `default_neuropixels`, `no_filter`. Whitening explicitly None -- whitening happens inside the sorter, not preprocessing.
3) RecordingSelection (v2/recording.py:652-725) -- Manual, PK `recording_id` (uuid); FKs Raw, SortGroupV2, IntervalList, PreprocessingParameters, LabTeam. `insert_selection` is find-existing-or-insert returning a PK-only dict; raises DuplicateSelectionError on multi-match.
4) Recording (v2/recording.py:771-...) -- Computed; tri-part with NamedTuples (RecordingFetched/RecordingComputed). Streams one ElectricalSeries into a fresh AnalysisNwbfile, validates saved timestamp coverage, hashes the file via NwbfileHasher.
5) ArtifactDetectionParameters (v2/artifact.py:94-147) -- Lookup; Pydantic-validated, defaults `"none"` and `"default"`, schema_version=2.
6) SharedArtifactGroup + Member (v2/artifact.py:151-...) -- Manual master/part; lets users union artifact detection across multiple Recordings of the same session (Spyglass issue #928).
7) ArtifactSelection (v2/artifact.py:363-...) -- Manual, PK `artifact_id` (uuid) + RecordingSource part xor SharedArtifactGroupSource part (source-part pattern). `insert_selection` enforces exactly-one source.
8) ArtifactDetection (v2/artifact.py:581-...) -- Computed; tri-part. Writes valid-times row(s) to common.IntervalList under `f"artifact_{artifact_id}"` (one row per session for shared groups).
9) SorterParameters (v2/sorting.py:75-198) -- Lookup; per-sorter Pydantic dispatch (`_get_sorter_schema`). Defaults for mountainsort4 (tetrode + cortex), mountainsort5, kilosort4, spykingcircus2, tridesclous2, clusterless_thresholder (with `noise_levels=[1.0]` and ClusterlessThresholderSchema schema_version=3).
10) SortingSelection (v2/sorting.py:201-...) -- Manual, PK `sorting_id` (uuid); FK SorterParameters + nullable FK ArtifactDetection + RecordingSource xor ConcatenatedRecordingSource part. Concat raises NotImplementedError today.
11) Sorting (v2/sorting.py:409-...) + Sorting.Unit part. Computed; tri-part. Builds a SpikeInterface SortingAnalyzer(format="binary_folder", sparse=True), computes random_spikes / noise_levels / templates / waveforms, writes curated units NWB, persists `analyzer_folder` + `n_units` + `time_of_sort`. Unit part carries peak Electrode FK + peak_amplitude_uv + n_spikes.
12) CurationV2 (v2/curation.py:39-...) + parts Unit, UnitLabel, MergeGroup. Manual; lineage via parent_curation_id (validation only). `insert_curation` (line 132) atomically inserts master + parts + the SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV2 merge-table row (spikesorting_merge.py:126-133), validates labels, enforces no empty/singleton merge groups, dedup-overlapping ids, idempotent root-curation reuse, supports `apply_merge` true/false (lazy preview).
13) SessionGroup / MotionCorrectionParameters / ConcatenatedRecordingSelection / ConcatenatedRecording (v2/session_group.py) -- all schemas are FINAL-SHAPE, `make()` and `insert_selection` raise NotImplementedError. Forward-declared so SortingSelection can FK them today without a future migration.
14) `run_v2_pipeline` (v2/pipeline.py:86-279) drives RecordingSelection -> Recording -> ArtifactSelection -> ArtifactDetection -> SortingSelection -> Sorting -> CurationV2 from one call, returns a manifest including the merge_id, and always emits a curation+merge row (even on zero-unit sorts unless require_units=True).

module_correspondence.note: v1 re-exports all tables eagerly; v2 deliberately exposes only initialize_v2_defaults to keep optional deps lazy.
Same electrode-grouping role; v2 adds inspect-before-destroy contract to fix v1's silent-recreate regression.
Lookup of preprocessing presets; v2 Pydantic-validates blob and adds params_schema_version column.
Same FK set + UUID PK; v2 helper is find-existing-or-insert returning PK-only dict.
Both Computed + tri-part; both persist to AnalysisNwbfile. v2 adds NamedTuple typing and explicit NwbfileHasher digest.
Same role; v2 schema-versioned and Pydantic-validated.
v2 adds source-part pattern (RecordingSource xor SharedArtifactGroupSource) for cross-recording artifact detection.
New v2 capability: union artifact detection across multiple recordings of same session (issue #928).
Same Computed role writing valid-times rows to IntervalList; v2 produces one IntervalList row per member session for shared groups.
Both Lookup; v2 per-sorter Pydantic schema dispatch. clusterless_thresholder default seed-pinned with explicit noise_levels=[1.0].
v2 makes artifact a real nullable FK to ArtifactDetection and adds RecordingSource xor ConcatenatedRecordingSource pattern; concat raises today.
Both Computed + tri-part. v2 builds a SortingAnalyzer (binary_folder, sparse) and persists analyzer_folder + n_units; v2 adds Unit part with peak Electrode FK.
v2 splits the NWB-blob fields into queryable part rows; insert_curation is atomic across master + parts + merge-table; restores merge provenance v1 buried inside the NWB.
Entire automated metric/auto-merge workflow is not ported; comment promises it 'added by the matching v2 change'. CurationV2 has metrics_source='analyzer_curation' placeholder.
No v2 equivalent; v1 burst-pair detection / xcorr asymmetry workflow has no port and no stub file.
Renamed Fig -> figpack; runtime not yet ported. CurationV2 reserves metrics_source='figpack' enum value.
No v2 equivalent. v2/recording.py instead carries an NwbfileHasher digest column inline on the Recording row (shares the v1 hashing path), but the SI-version recompute audit pipeline has no port.
New cross-session / concat scaffolding -- final-shape schemas with make()/insert_selection raising NotImplementedError until the materializer lands.
New end-to-end orchestrator with 3 named presets; idempotent; emits a manifest including the merge_id.
Cross-session unit-tracking and pluggable matcher protocol are forward-declared with 3-line stubs.
Both pipelines feed the same merge table via dedicated part tables; v2's part is only declared when v2 is importable.

known_gaps: MetricCuration (v1/metric_curation.py 710 lines: WaveformParameters, MetricParameters, MetricCurationParameters, MetricCurationSelection, MetricCuration with waveform cache + quality-metric compute + auto-label/merge) -- v2/metric_curation.py is a 3-line stub at line 1-3.
FigURLCuration (v1/figurl_curation.py 322 lines: kachery-cloud / sortingview-based interactive curation URL generation) -- v2/figpack_curation.py is a 3-line stub (renamed Fig -> figpack).

BurstPair / BurstPairParams / BurstPairSelection (v1/burst_curation.py 397 lines: burst-parent vs burst-child detection via cross-correlogram asymmetry, ISI violations, waveform similarity, with diagnostic plots) -- no v2 module and no stub; entire feature absent.

RecordingRecompute / RecordingRecomputeVersions / RecordingRecomputeSelection (v1/recompute.py 981 lines: tracks pynwb / spikeinterface dependency versions, replays preprocessing, hash-diffs against original AnalysisNwbfile) -- no v2 equivalent; v2 keeps only the inline NwbfileHasher digest column.
Cross-session unit matching (v2/unit_matching.py + v2/matcher_protocol.py 3-line stubs) -- v1 also has no equivalent, but v2 reserves this as a planned feature with stubs.

ConcatenatedRecording materializer (v2/session_group.py: ConcatenatedRecording.make() and ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection raise NotImplementedError) -- schema is final-shape but consumer logic is not implemented. SortingSelection's ConcatenatedRecordingSource path is correspondingly gated with NotImplementedError in Sorting.make.
SessionGroup helpers (SessionGroup.create_group, SessionGroup.is_multi_day) raise NotImplementedError in v2/session_group.py:69-96.

Mountainsort4-on-modern-SpikeInterface guard: v1 ArtifactDetection and v1 MetricCuration call _require_legacy_si_environment (v1/artifact.py:152, v1/metric_curation.py:17) -- v2 has no equivalent legacy-SI gate because it targets the modern SortingAnalyzer API.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `set_group_by_shank` supports per-electrode-group references; v2 only allows a single reference for all sort groups
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Accepts `references: dict` keyed by electrode-group name (lines 50-95). Falls back to per-shank `electrodes['original_reference_electrode']` from the Electrode config (utils.py:84-93). Each shank's sort_reference_electrode_id can therefore differ.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Single int kwarg `sort_reference_electrode_id` applied to every sort group it creates (lines 233-273). Explicitly documents 'Per-group references are not supported in v2; if you need them, build the rows manually'. Never consults `original_reference_electrode`.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring explicitly says per-group references not supported, but no rationale is given for dropping this v1 production capability. The Electrode table still has `original_reference_electrode` populated per shank for Frank-lab tetrode rigs — v1 used it by default. v2 silently throws that information away and the global -1/-2/single-id model cannot reproduce v1's default Frank-lab behavior automatically.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale beyond 'not supported'

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the cited code in both versions.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:50-95): `set_group_by_shank(cls, nwb_file_name, references: dict = None, omit_ref_electrode_group=False, omit_unitrode=True)` — accepts a `references: dict` with the docstring saying "Keys: electrode groups. Values: reference electrode." It delegates to `get_group_by_shank` in utils.py.

v1 fallback (src/spyglass/spikesorting/utils.py:76-93): when `references` is None, the loop iterates per (electrode_group, shank) and reads `electrodes['original_reference_electrode'][match_names_bool]`, asserting the values are uniform across the shank, then uses that as `sort_ref_id`. So each shank's `sort_reference_electrode_id` can differ.

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:232-273): signature is `set_group_by_shank(cls, nwb_file_name, omit_ref_electrode_group=False, omit_unitrode=True, sort_reference_electrode_id: int = -1, sort_group_ids=None, delete_existing_entries=False, confirm=False)`. The docstring at lines 259-263 says: "Reference electrode applied to every sort group this call creates. ``-1`` = none; ``-2`` = global median; ``>=0`` = specific channel id. Per-group references are not supported in v2; if you need them, build the rows manually." At line 379 the same scalar `sort_reference_electrode_id` is written into every master row.

I grepped v2 for `original_reference_electrode` and found zero hits — v2 never reads it. I grepped v2 tests for `references=` calls and found zero — no test exercises per-group references (none exist to test). Tests at test_single_session_pipeline.py:5224-5292 cover only the global -1/-2 path.

The v2 module-level docstring (recording.py:1-15) gives no rationale for dropping per-group references. The class docstring and `set_group_by_shank` docstring just state "not supported" without justification. No "WHY" comment is anywhere near the call site.

Mitigations: (a) v2 SortGroupV2 schema (line 118) still stores `sort_reference_electrode_id` per master row, so the data model supports per-group refs — only the constructor is restricted. (b) Callers can manually build rows or call `set_group_by_shank` once per intended reference. (c) `set_group_by_electrode_table_column` exists as an alternate constructor. But the default Frank-lab tetrode behavior (auto-pick per-shank reference from Electrode config) is silently lost: a user porting from v1 who relied on the default will now write -1 (no reference) into every sort group unless they notice the API change.

Classification: intentional_unjustified — the v2 author was clearly aware of the v1 behavior (docstring explicitly mentions per-group references are unsupported), so this is deliberate, but no documented rationale or migration guide is provided.

Severity: high is defensible because silently writing the wrong reference electrode for every sort group on a production Frank-lab tetrode pipeline would propagate into the preprocessed Recording (line 379 plus referencing applied during `Recording.make` per the module docstring lines 12-14) and could subtly bias spike sorting outcomes. The user is not warned that the v1 default no longer fires.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 inserts an IntervalList row for the intersected sort interval; v2 inserts no IntervalList row from Recording.make
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: make_fetch/make_insert insert the sort_interval_valid_times into common.IntervalList under interval_list_name=recording_id (recording.py:212-216, 255-257).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Recording.make has no IntervalList insert; the saved range is recorded only on the Recording row (saved_start, saved_end, duration_s). No `recording_id`-keyed IntervalList row exists for downstream queries.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Grep across v2 confirms no IntervalList.insert from recording.py; only artifact.py inserts (artifact.py:825). v1's `recording_id`-keyed IntervalList was a publicly-queryable provenance row — any external code that did `IntervalList & {interval_list_name: recording_id}` would now fetch empty.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale (recording.py module docstring at v2/recording.py:1-15 does not mention this drop)

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the divergence holds in code.

V1 confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:
- Lines 211-216: `sort_interval_valid_times.set_key(nwb_file_name=..., interval_list_name=key["recording_id"], pipeline="spikesorting_recording_v1")`
- Lines 255-257: `IntervalList.insert1(sort_interval_valid_times.as_dict, skip_duplicates=True)` inside `make_insert`.

V2 confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:
- `make_insert` (lines 990-1100) only calls `AnalysisNwbfile().add(nwb_file_name, analysis_file_name)` and `self.insert1(...)` on the Recording row itself. No `IntervalList.insert1`.
- The Recording row stores `saved_start`/`saved_end` (computed but only `duration_s` is persisted at line 1085 along with `n_channels`, `sampling_frequency`, `cache_hash`) -- the requested range is enforced via a truncation check (line 1042-1068) but never written to IntervalList.

Grep `IntervalList\.\(insert\|insert1\)` across all v2 returns only artifact.py:825 -- confirming no Recording-side IntervalList insert.

Module docstring (v2/recording.py:1-15) and the Recording class docstring (lines 773-792) do not mention dropping the IntervalList row; no documented rationale.

Caveat that softens severity: searching `interval_list_name.*recording_id` across `src/spyglass/` finds NO internal consumer in either v1 or v2. v1 sorting.py:263 reads `recording_key["interval_list_name"]` from the join with SpikeSortingSelection (the user-supplied artifact_id row, not the recording_id row). So the v1 `recording_id`-keyed IntervalList row was provenance only -- queryable externally via `IntervalList & {interval_list_name: recording_id}` but not consumed by internal pipeline code. External users relying on that provenance row would get empty results in v2.

Grep of tests/spikesorting/v2/ confirms no test asserts the IntervalList row's presence or absence (only `baseline_capture.py:421-427` reads the `artifact_id`-keyed row, not the `recording_id`-keyed one).

Classification: intentional_unjustified (real divergence, design choice to store range on Recording row, no documented rationale).
Severity: medium per reviewer, but I'd lean low-to-medium because no internal code paths consume the dropped row -- only external queries against IntervalList by recording_id would be affected. Going with medium per reviewer since external query surface is a real backward-compat concern and the row was the only public IntervalList provenance for the preprocessed recording.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 fixes a v1 off-by-one bug in `_consolidate_intervals` that silently dropped the last sample of every disjoint interval
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `_consolidate_intervals` uses `stop_indices = np.searchsorted(timestamps, intervals[:,1], side='right') - 1`, returning an inclusive stop index, then passes it as `frame_slice(end_frame=stop)` which is EXCLUSIVE — dropping the last sample (~33 us at 30 kHz) of each disjoint chunk.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Uses `side='right'` directly as exclusive end (utils.py:480-488). Adjusts join condition to `next_start <= stop` (was `>= next_start - 1` in v1). The v2 docstring explicitly documents the v1 bug.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring (utils.py:440-450) names the v1 bug. Test coverage exists in test_single_session_pipeline.py:2920 etc. for off-by-one boundary.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented at utils.py:440-450

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the claim by reading the cited code directly.

V1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:741-744):
```
start_indices = np.searchsorted(timestamps, intervals[:, 0], side="left")
stop_indices = (
    np.searchsorted(timestamps, intervals[:, 1], side="right") - 1
)
```
V1 join condition at line 753: `if stop >= next_start - 1:` (consistent with inclusive stop).

V1 then uses those indices as `end_frame=` at recording.py:567-579 inside `frame_slice(...)` / array slicing `all_timestamps[start:stop]`. Python slicing and SpikeInterface's `frame_slice` both treat `end_frame` as EXCLUSIVE (confirmed by reading /cumulus/edeno/clusterless_high_d/.venv/.../spikeinterface/core/baserecording.py:240-282, where `end_frame` is documented as the standard half-open endpoint). So v1 silently drops the last sample of each disjoint interval — ~33 us at 30 kHz.

V2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/utils.py:480-488):
```
start_indices = _np.searchsorted(timestamps, intervals[:, 0], side="left")
stop_indices = _np.searchsorted(timestamps, intervals[:, 1], side="right")  # no -1
```
V2 join condition at line 499: `if next_start <= stop:` (exclusive-end semantics).

V2 docstring at utils.py:440-450 explicitly names the v1 bug and the fix: "v1 computed stop_indices = searchsorted(side='right') - 1 (an INCLUSIVE end index), then passed that value to frame_slice(end_frame=stop) whose end_frame is EXCLUSIVE. Net effect: v1 silently dropped the last sample (~33 us at 30 kHz) of every disjoint interval."

Test coverage exists for the boundary in tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:2910-2925 ("Also pins the off-by-one boundary: the LAST artifact frame must be zeroed..."), and many other off-by-one boundary references throughout that file (lines 1260, 1828, 2814, 2993, 3007, 3019, 3946, 5582).

Classification `intentional_justified` is correct: rationale is documented in the docstring and exercised by tests. Severity `low` is correct: a single dropped frame per disjoint sort interval has negligible effect on spike-sorting outcomes (a 33 us gap at 30 kHz, dwarfed by typical artifact/refractory windows and well below any detection threshold's temporal extent).

confirmed_findings.title: v1 silently selects gains[0] for heterogeneous channel gains; v2 raises ValueError
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `conversion=np.unique(recording.get_channel_gains())[0] * 1e-6` (line 858) — silently picks the first unique gain even when multiple exist, mis-scaling channels.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Raises ValueError 'heterogeneous channel gains' when more than one unique gain is detected (lines 1862-1869).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Test coverage at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3139-3175. Inline rationale at v2/recording.py:1854-1861 references shared-contracts.md and identifies v1 'pick first' as a latent correctness bug.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented as a deliberate correctness fix

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all four pillars of the claim by reading the cited code directly:

1. v1/recording.py:858 contains exactly: `conversion=np.unique(recording.get_channel_gains())[0] * 1e-6`. This silently picks index [0] of the unique gains array with no length check — confirmed latent bug if a sort group ever contains heterogeneous gains.

2. v2/recording.py:1862-1870 contains:
```
gains = _np.unique(recording.get_channel_gains())
if len(gains) != 1:
    raise ValueError(
        "Recording.make: recording has heterogeneous channel "
        f"gains {gains.tolist()}; v2 ElectricalSeries write "
        "requires a single conversion factor. Verify probe "
        "metadata for the sort group."
    )
conversion = float(gains[0]) * 1e-6
```

3. Inline rationale at v2/recording.py:1853-1861 explicitly names v1's behavior at v1/recording.py:858 as a "latent correctness bug" because "the chosen gain becomes the universal conversion factor on the ElectricalSeries, so signals on channels with heterogeneous gains are scaled by the wrong factor." References shared-contracts.md.

4. Test coverage confirmed at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3138-3179: test `test_recording_make_rejects_heterogeneous_gains` builds a `si.NumpyRecording` with `set_channel_gains([0.195, 0.195, 0.5, 0.5])` and asserts `pytest.raises(ValueError, match="heterogeneous channel")` against `Recording._write_nwb_artifact`. The test docstring explicitly notes "The check is in place at recording.py but had no test; this regression test ... pins the raise."

The divergence is a deliberate, documented, tested correctness fix. Classification intentional_justified is correct. Severity medium is appropriate: the bug only manifests on probes/sort groups with non-uniform gains (uncommon in clean Trodes setups but possible with mixed probe types), and the consequence is mis-scaled voltages flowing into all downstream amplitude/threshold/uV computations — incorrect but not catastrophic and not guaranteed.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 preprocessing param schema includes `margin_ms` and `seed`; v2 drops both fields entirely with `extra='forbid'`
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Default preset has `margin_ms: 5` and `seed: 0` (lines 127-138) — declared in schema but never referenced in code (grep confirms no usage).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `PreprocessingParamsSchema` has no `seed`, no `margin_ms`. `ConfigDict(extra='forbid')` rejects any blob containing them (preprocessing.py:21,55,70,95).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Inline v2 commentary at recording.py:586-591 documents schema_version bump for 'min_segment_length' added + 'reference' field removed; does not mention seed/margin but their dropping is consistent with the dead-field cleanup. Migration path for a stored v1 params blob is now broken (would fail validation).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Implicit cleanup of dead v1 fields; not individually documented but consistent with v2 schema-version=2 cleanup

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the divergence is real and the classification holds.

V1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:127-138):
```
contents = [["default", {"frequency_min": 300, "frequency_max": 6000,
    "margin_ms": 5, "seed": 0, "min_segment_length": 1}]]
```
Confirmed `margin_ms` and `seed` are dead: grep finds them only in this schema row and the docstring (line 112). The v1 consumer `_get_preprocessed_recording` (line 540-626) only reads `filter_params["frequency_min"]` and `filter_params["frequency_max"]` — `margin_ms` and `seed` are never indexed.

V2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/_params/preprocessing.py:74-110):
```
class PreprocessingParamsSchema(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")
    schema_version: int = 2
    bandpass_filter: BandpassFilterParams = ...
    common_reference: CommonReferenceParams = ...
    whiten: WhitenParams | None = ...
    min_segment_length: float = Field(default=1.0, ge=0.0)
```
No `seed`, no `margin_ms`. `extra="forbid"` at line 95 means a v1 blob containing them would raise ValidationError.

Documented rationale: v2/recording.py:1-15 declares a "zero-migration policy" so the broken migration path is by design, not oversight. The v2 inline commentary at recording.py:586-591 documents the schema_version=2 bump (specifically calling out `min_segment_length` added + `reference` field removed) but does not individually mention seed/margin_ms. However, tests/spikesorting/v2/_parity_canonical.py:253-257 explicitly documents: "v1's `seed` and `margin_ms` are dropped (v2 hardcodes both in the runtime path, so they are schema-only on v1)." This is the missing per-field justification.

Tests: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_params_validation.py exercises PreprocessingParamsSchema (defaults round-trip, inverted bandpass rejection, whiten=None) and the parametrized test_schema_version_present_and_positive. No direct test for rejection of legacy `seed`/`margin_ms` blobs, but the `extra="forbid"` behavior is verified for a sibling schema (test_artifact_rejects_unknown_field at line 103-108).

Classification intentional_justified is correct: dead v1 fields removed under documented zero-migration policy with per-field rationale (in the parity test). Severity low is correct: dropping fields that were never consumed at runtime cannot change scientific output.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 drops `electrodes_id` column and adds new metadata columns
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: SpikeSortingRecording has `electrodes_id=null: varchar(40)` (separate object_id for the ProcessedElectricalSeries/electrodes sub-object) and `hash=null: varchar(32)` (line 193-194).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Recording schema (lines 794-806) has no `electrodes_id`; carries instead `electrical_series_path`, `n_channels`, `sampling_frequency`, `duration_s`, `cache_hash char(64)`, `timestamps_adjusted`, `n_adjusted_samples`. `object_id` is widened to varchar(72).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 schema captures persistent provenance (cache_hash, timestamps_adjusted, n_adjusted_samples) and resolution path (electrical_series_path). Tests cover the new columns (test_single_session_pipeline.py:351-378, 5598-5616). External tooling that read `electrodes_id` from v1 has no migration path.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented at v2/recording.py:771-792 (cache_hash matches shared-contracts.md; electrical_series_path commentary at line 1118-1123)

verdict.reasoning:
Verified directly against source:

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:185-195):
```
class SpikeSortingRecording(SpyglassMixin, dj.Computed):
    definition = """
    # Processed recording.
    -> SpikeSortingRecordingSelection
    ---
    -> AnalysisNwbfile
    object_id: varchar(40) # Object ID for the processed recording in NWB file
    electrodes_id=null: varchar(40) # Object ID for the processed electrodes
    hash=null: varchar(32) # Hash of the NWB file
    """
```

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:794-806):
```
definition = """
-> RecordingSelection
---
-> AnalysisNwbfile
electrical_series_path: varchar(255)
object_id: varchar(72)
n_channels: int
sampling_frequency: float
duration_s: float
cache_hash: char(64)
timestamps_adjusted=0: bool   # source recording's timestamps were repaired
n_adjusted_samples=0: int     # source-wide count of repaired samples
"""
```

The divergence is real and matches the reviewer's summary exactly. v2 drops `electrodes_id` and `hash` (sized varchar(32)), replaces with `cache_hash char(64)`, widens `object_id` to varchar(72), and adds `electrical_series_path`, `n_channels`, `sampling_frequency`, `duration_s`, `timestamps_adjusted`, `n_adjusted_samples`.

Rationale is documented:
- v2/recording.py:771-792 docstring explains the cache_hash and tri-part dispatch design, referencing shared-contracts "Recording Cache Format"
- v2/recording.py:1118-1123 explains why electrical_series_path is explicit (avoid SI auto-detect picking wrong ElectricalSeries when multiple are present)

Tests confirmed:
- test_single_session_pipeline.py:351-378 asserts the new columns are populated correctly
- test_single_session_pipeline.py:5598-5616 specifically verifies cache_hash matches NwbfileHasher
- test_single_session_pipeline.py:1432-1504 covers timestamps_adjusted/n_adjusted_samples
- test_single_session_pipeline.py:5916-5946 verifies get_recording honors electrical_series_path

Classification intentional_justified is correct: design is documented, tested, and serves clear correctness purposes (provenance via cache_hash, timestamp-repair provenance, explicit series resolution to prevent SI ambiguity). The dropped `electrodes_id` reflects a different artifact model in v2 — get_recording uses electrical_series_path with read_nwb_recording rather than referencing a separate electrodes sub-object.

Severity medium is appropriate: external tooling that read v1's `electrodes_id` has no migration path, but this is a v2-wide schema redesign rather than a silent correctness issue.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `set_group_by_shank` silently deletes/re-inserts on rerun; v2 refuses by default and requires explicit opt-in
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: If existing rows for nwb_file_name, `(SortGroup & ...).delete()` and re-insert without warning (lines 81-95).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `_handle_existing` (lines 150-228) refuses by default. Caller must either pass explicit non-overlapping sort_group_ids (additive) or `delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=True` after viewing `preview_existing_entries`.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring (lines 99-112) explicitly names this as fixing a v1 regression that cascaded deletes. Test coverage at test_single_session_pipeline.py:80-145, 2105-2135.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in SortGroupV2 class docstring lines 99-112

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all three claims against the actual code.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:81-95) confirms silent delete-and-reinsert:
```
existing_entries = SortGroup & {"nwb_file_name": nwb_file_name}
if existing_entries and test_mode:
    return
elif existing_entries:
    # delete any current groups
    (SortGroup & {"nwb_file_name": nwb_file_name}).delete()
```
No warning, no preview, no confirmation. The only escape is `test_mode=True`, which silently no-ops.

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:150-228) confirms the inspect-before-destroy contract in `_handle_existing`:
- Default `delete_existing_entries=False`: refuses with ValueError unless caller passes explicit non-overlapping `sort_group_ids` (lines 196-218).
- `delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=False`: raises ValueError that includes the `DeletionPreview` (lines 220-226).
- Only `delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=True` proceeds, and uses `cautious_delete()` (line 228), not raw `.delete()`.
- Also rejects duplicate ids in explicit lists (lines 176-190).

Rationale is documented in v2 SortGroupV2 class docstring at lines 103-111, explicitly naming the v1 regression: "This fixes a v1 regression where `set_group_by_shank` silently dropped and re-created sort groups on rerun, cascading deletes through every downstream Sorting and CurationV1 row without warning."

Test coverage confirmed in tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:
- line 80 `test_set_group_by_shank_refuses_overlapping_rerun`
- line 93 `test_set_group_by_shank_overwrite_requires_confirm`
- line 2105 `test_handle_existing_rejects_duplicate_sort_group_ids`

Classification intentional_justified is correct: the v2 author explicitly names this as a v1 regression fix, the cascade-on-rerun is a real data-integrity hazard (deletes ripple to Sorting/CurationV1), and the new API still allows the old behavior via opt-in. Severity "high" is appropriate because the v1 behavior could silently destroy downstream curation work.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `get_group_by_shank` crashes on non-numeric `electrode_group_name`; v2 normalizes
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `e_groups.sort(key=int)` (utils.py:50) raises ValueError if any group name is non-decimal (e.g., 'probeA').
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Uses `_electrode_group_sort_key` (lines 75-87) which routes non-decimal names to a secondary lexical sort.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring (lines 76-87) explicitly explains the fix. Test coverage at test_single_session_pipeline.py:2081-...
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in function docstring

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all four claim elements directly against source:

1. v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/utils.py:50 reads exactly `e_groups.sort(key=int)  # sort electrode groups numerically`. `int("probeA")` raises ValueError, so non-decimal `electrode_group_name` values crash `get_group_by_shank`.

2. v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:75-87 defines `_electrode_group_sort_key(name)` with body `text = str(name); return (0, int(text)) if text.isdecimal() else (1, text)`. The docstring (lines 76-85) explicitly explains: numeric names sort numerically and ahead of non-numeric names; non-numeric names sort lexically; `isdecimal` is used as the gate because it matches exactly the strings `int()` accepts. Rationale is explicitly documented.

3. The function is actually wired in: recording.py:287-290 uses `sorted(np.unique(electrodes["electrode_group_name"]).tolist(), key=_electrode_group_sort_key)` inside `set_group_by_shank`, matching v1's call site role.

4. Test coverage exists at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:2080-2102 (`test_electrode_group_sort_key_tolerates_non_numeric`). It asserts: (a) numeric strings sort numerically not lexically: `["10","2","1"] -> ["1","2","10"]`; (b) mixed inputs don't raise and non-numeric names sort after numeric: `["probeB","2","probeA","1"] -> ["1","2","probeA","probeB"]`.

The divergence is real, intentional, justified by docstring, and tested. Severity is low because the v1 crash only triggers for non-decimal `electrode_group_name` values, which are uncommon in NWB files in practice (typical values are numeric strings like "0", "1", "2"), and it fails loudly (ValueError) rather than silently producing wrong outputs.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 supports `rounding`/`precision_lookup` knob on hash; v2 hardcodes default precision (4 decimals) with no user control
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `_make_file` exposes `rounding: int = 4` parameter passed through to `AnalysisNwbfile().get_hash(..., precision_lookup=dict(ProcessedElectricalSeries=rounding))` (lines 275-300, 352-357).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `_hash_nwb_recording` calls `AnalysisNwbfile().get_hash(analysis_file_name)` with no precision_lookup, relying on the module default `PRECISION_LOOKUP = dict(ProcessedElectricalSeries=4)`.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Default values match (4 decimals), so common-path output is identical. The flexibility loss is undocumented; users with non-uV-precision recordings (e.g., low-amplitude LFP) cannot tighten the digest.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale; v2 just simplifies the API

verdict.reasoning:
Verified at the cited lines.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:270-300, 352-357): `_make_file` declares `rounding: int = 4` (line 275) with docstring at lines 297-300 explicitly documenting it as "Decimal places to round to when hashing. Default 4, which is typical for microvolt precision. Only used for hash computation, does not affect data written to NWB file." It is threaded into the hash call at line 355: `precision_lookup=dict(ProcessedElectricalSeries=rounding)`.

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/utils.py:571-590): `_hash_nwb_recording(analysis_file_name)` calls `AnalysisNwbfile().get_hash(analysis_file_name)` at line 590 with no precision_lookup kwarg. Confirmed at src/spyglass/utils/nwb_hash.py:18 that the module default is `PRECISION_LOOKUP = dict(ProcessedElectricalSeries=4)` and at nwb_hash.py:194-195 that None falls back to this default. So v2 defaults to 4 decimals exactly like v1's default, matching the claim.

v2 caller (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:1923) hardcodes `_hash_nwb_recording(analysis_file_name)` with no kwarg threading. The docstring at lines 1798-1803 says "v1 recompute machinery uses the same hashing path" but does not justify removing the rounding knob.

Additional context: v1's `rounding` is not a dead internal kwarg — it is exposed as a DataJoint table parameter in v1/recompute.py:193 (`rounding=4: int`) and threaded through recompute.py:718. v2 lacks a recompute pipeline so far, so the loss only matters when v2's recompute story lands.

v2 tests: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:5599-5620 only verifies `Recording.cache_hash` equals `_hash_nwb_recording(analysis_file_name)`. No test varies precision/rounding, so the branch where precision != 4 has zero coverage. This is expected since the knob no longer exists in v2.

Common path is byte-identical (both default to 4). The flexibility loss is real but minor and only matters if v2's eventual recompute pipeline wants per-recording-type precision tuning. Severity stays low and classification is best described as drift toward simplification with no documented rationale - closer to unintentional_drift than intentional_justified, but since v2 hashing happens in a different architectural place (post-write helper, not configurable factory), this is plausibly intentional simplification. Keeping classification as uncertain matches the reviewer's call.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 always-on monotonicity repair of source timestamps; v1 has no equivalent
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Calls `recording.get_times()` directly (line 554). No detection or repair of non-monotonic timestamps. Downstream `np.searchsorted` in `_consolidate_intervals` returns wrong indices on stitched epochs with floating-point artifacts.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `_repaired_timestamps` (lines 1546-1636) detects via chunked monotonicity check, repairs via shifted cummax, logs warning, and persists provenance (`timestamps_adjusted`, `n_adjusted_samples`).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring (lines 1568-1571) calls out as defensive port of upstream `copilot/fix-populating-artifact-detection` branch (commits d5079a8e / 963aa915). Test coverage at test_v1_parity.py:403-498 and test_single_session_pipeline.py:1518-1579.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented as defensive port

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all claims by reading the cited code.

v1 at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:551-554:
```
recording = se.read_nwb_recording(nwb_file_abs_path, load_time_vector=True)
all_timestamps = recording.get_times()
```
No monotonicity check, no repair. A grep for `monoton|cummax|maximum.accumulate|adjusted` against v1/recording.py returns zero hits.

v2 at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:1546-1636 implements `_repaired_timestamps` exactly as claimed: chunked monotonicity check (`_count_non_monotonic_chunked`), early-return fast path when `n_issues == 0`, shifted cummax repair (`buffer = ts - i*sp; np.maximum.accumulate(buffer, out=buffer); buffer += i*sp`), warning log, and returns `(timestamps, n_changed)`.

Docstring at lines 1568-1571 explicitly states: "Defensive port of the in-flight v1 fix from the upstream `copilot/fix-populating-artifact-detection` branch (commits d5079a8e / 963aa915)."

Provenance is persisted: schema declares `timestamps_adjusted: bool` (line 767, default 0 at line 804) and `n_adjusted_samples: int` (line 768, default 0 at line 805); wired through `RecordingComputed` at lines 1245-1249 and saved in row dict at lines 1087-1088.

Test coverage confirmed:
- tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:403 and :498 call `Recording._repaired_timestamps` directly.
- tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:1518 defines `test_recording_single_interval_persists_repaired_timestamps`.
- test_single_session_pipeline.py:1485-1504 verifies row persistence by monkeypatching `_repaired_timestamps`.

The divergence is real, has documented rationale (defensive port of in-flight upstream fix), and has test coverage. Classification `new_in_v2` is correct — v2 adds defensive correctness logic that v1 master lacks. Severity `medium` is appropriate: the bug it guards against silently shifts sort-interval frame indices via `np.searchsorted` in `_consolidate_intervals` on epoch-stitched recordings with FP-precision timestamp artifacts; could also crash `ArtifactDetection.add_removal_window` with `start > stop`. Not critical because it requires specific epoch-stitching conditions, but more than low because data integrity is at stake silently.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 RecordingTruncatedError fires when saved duration < requested chunk total; v1 silently writes whatever survives
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: No coverage check between requested `valid_times` and saved data. A frame_slice that excludes data due to interval misalignment writes a short ElectricalSeries with no error.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: make_insert computes `requested_total = sum(end - start for start, end in sort_valid_times)` vs `saved_total = saved_end - saved_start`. If `missing > 1.5/fs`, raises RecordingTruncatedError after unlinking the file (lines 1036-1068).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Inline rationale at v2/recording.py:1019-1023 ties this to Spyglass #1585. Test coverage at test_single_session_pipeline.py:1224-1402.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented as #1585 fix

verdict.reasoning:
Verified directly:

v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:241-261 — `make_insert` does only `IntervalList.insert1(...)`, `AnalysisNwbfile().add(...)`, `self.insert1(...)`, `self._record_environment(...)`. No coverage comparison between requested `sort_interval_valid_times` and the saved data. Truncation/short-write would proceed silently.

v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:1042-1068 — explicitly computes:
```
requested_total = float(sum(end - start for start, end in sort_valid_times))
saved_total = float(saved_end - saved_start)
tolerance = 1.5 / sampling_frequency
missing = requested_total - saved_total
if missing > tolerance:
    ... unlink abs_path ...
    raise RecordingTruncatedError(...)
```
Matches the reviewer's described mechanic exactly (sum-of-chunk-durations vs saved envelope, 1.5/fs tolerance, unlink-before-raise).

Documented rationale: v2/recording.py:1016-1023 docstring cites #1585 and explicitly explains the 1.5-sample tolerance handling the `(N-1)/fs` NWB boundary, plus the disjoint-chunk reasoning at lines 1035-1041 (saved envelope excludes inter-chunk gaps, so envelope comparison would spuriously flag every R5 disjoint sort).

Cleanup: lines 1052-1059 unlink the orphan analysis file with try/except before raising — matches claim.

Test coverage confirmed: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:1224 ("Validation goal #5"), 1297, 1318, 1402 explicitly use `pytest.raises(RecordingTruncatedError, ...)` and line 1567 verifies the negative case (no spurious raise within tolerance).

RecordingTruncatedError class exists at v2/exceptions.py:25 — no v1 analog.

Classification new_in_v2 is correct: this is a genuinely new defensive guard introduced in v2 with documented #1585 justification, not a port of v1 behavior. Severity medium is reasonable — a missing guard in v1 could let short ElectricalSeries propagate downstream (medium impact); the v2 guard is a correctness improvement, not a regression.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `omit_ref_electrode_group` uses per-shank `original_reference_electrode`; v2 uses single passed-in `sort_reference_electrode_id`
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: For each shank, looks up the shank's actual reference electrode (`shank_elect_ref = electrodes['original_reference_electrode'][match_names_bool]`) and identifies which electrode group it belongs to; if that group equals the current group, the shank is omitted (utils.py:84-116).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Checks the passed-in `sort_reference_electrode_id` against the electrode table. If the param is -1 (default), `ref_match.any()` returns False and `omit_ref_electrode_group` is silently a no-op. Only the group containing the passed-in id is omitted (lines 313-335).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 has no test calling `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` (grep returns no hits in tests/spikesorting/v2). The default code path `set_group_by_shank(nwb_file_name=...)` uses ref_id=-1, so passing `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` is effectively a no-op — a clear silent behavior shift from v1.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale; flag preserved as a kwarg but its semantics changed

verdict.reasoning: Verified at file:line. v1 utils.py:83-94 sets `sort_ref_id` from per-shank `original_reference_electrode` table column when no `references` dict is passed: `shank_elect_ref = electrodes["original_reference_electrode"][match_names_bool]`. Then v1 utils.py:97-116 computes `ref_elec_group` from that per-shank reference and skips the shank if `str(e_group) == str(ref_elec_group)`. v2 recording.py:313-335 takes a completely different path: it checks the SINGLE passed-in `sort_reference_electrode_id` kwarg (default -1 per v2 recording.py:238) against the electrode table via `ref_match = electrodes["electrode_id"] == (sort_reference_electrode_id)`. When the default -1 is used, no electrode matches (electrode_ids are non-negative), `ref_match.any()` is False, and the `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` branch becomes a silent no-op. v2 NEVER reads `original_reference_electrode` in `set_group_by_shank`. Test coverage: grep of tests/spikesorting/ for `omit_ref_electrode_group` returns zero hits — the flag is untested in v2 (and in v1 tests too). v2 docstring at recording.py:259-263 does document the intentional single-reference simplification: "Per-group references are not supported in v2; if you need them, build the rows manually." This justifies the design shift from per-shank references but does NOT warn the user that `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` combined with the default `sort_reference_electrode_id=-1` silently does nothing. So the broader semantic change is documented (single-reference model) but the silent-no-op consequence for the omit flag is not. The flag is preserved without a warning when it cannot fire.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `insert_selection` returns full row list on duplicates; v2 returns single PK-only dict consistently
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `if query: return query.fetch(as_dict=True)` — returns a list of full row dicts when an existing row matches (line 176-179). On insert, returns the modified input dict (with `recording_id` added).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Always returns single PK-only dict `{recording_id: ...}` (lines 698-725). Raises DuplicateSelectionError on multi-match (integrity bug).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring (lines 672-700) calls out shared-contracts.md 'insert_selection() Return-Value Normalization' convention. Code that relied on v1's list-of-dicts on duplicate (e.g., iterating return) will break.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented under shared-contracts

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all claims by reading the cited code directly.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:176-182):
```
query = cls & key
if query:
    logger.warning("Similar row(s) already inserted.")
    return query.fetch(as_dict=True)  # list[dict] of FULL rows
key["recording_id"] = uuid.uuid4()
cls.insert1(key, skip_duplicates=True)
return key  # input dict mutated with recording_id
```
Confirmed: returns list-of-full-row-dicts on duplicate, mutated input key on fresh insert. Inconsistent return type.

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:672-725):
```
existing = (cls & keys_minus_uuid).fetch("KEY", as_dict=True)
if len(existing) == 1:
    return existing[0]  # PK-only
if len(existing) > 1:
    raise DuplicateSelectionError(...)
...
cls.insert1(new_key)
return {k: new_key[k] for k in cls.primary_key}  # PK-only
```
Confirmed: always returns single PK-only dict {recording_id: uuid} or raises.

Documented rationale confirmed at .claude/docs/plans/spikesorting-v2/shared-contracts.md:527-574 ("insert_selection() Return-Value Normalization"). Quote line 529: "v1's insert_selection helpers return dict on fresh insert but list[dict] on rerun... This breaks downstream **rec_key splatting." Line 574 declares it an "invariant -- do not weaken... the single biggest UX win over v1 and is enforced by tests."

Test coverage confirmed at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:213-219, which asserts pk_first == pk_second, isinstance(pk_first, dict), and list(pk_first.keys()) == ["recording_id"]. Also tests at lines 599-621 for ArtifactSelection.

Classification intentional_justified is correct: divergence is documented in shared-contracts.md, justified as a UX/correctness improvement over v1's inconsistent return, and enforced by tests. Severity medium is appropriate -- it is a breaking API contract change for callers that iterate or splat v1's list return, but not a silent-correctness/data divergence.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 default preprocessing preset has one row; v2 ships three (default_franklab, default_neuropixels, no_filter)

confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `_DEFAULT_CONTENTS` contains three Pydantic-validated rows: `default_franklab` (whiten=None, defaults), `default_neuropixels` (300-6000, whiten=None), `no_filter` (1.0-14999.0, whiten=None). Each with `params_schema_version=2`.

The 'default' preset name from v1 no longer exists; downstream code referencing `preproc_params_name='default'` (Spyglass v1 production) must be migrated to `'default_franklab'`. No automatic migration path provided.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented for schema_version=2 / whiten=None alignment with sorter-side whitening

Verified directly in code.

v1 at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:127-138:
```
contents = [
    [
        "default",
        {
            "frequency_min": 300,

```
(Reviewer's summary said freq_min/freq_max; actual v1 uses frequency_min/frequency_max — minor inaccuracy but the structural claim of a single 'default' row is correct.)

v2 at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:592-634 ships three rows in `_DEFAULT_CONTENTS`: 'default_franklab' (whiten=None, otherwise schema defaults), 'default_neuropixels' (300-6000 bandpass, whiten=None), and 'no_filter' (1.0-14999.0 bandpass, whiten=None). Each row carries `params_schema_version=2` and `job_kwargs=None`.

Rationale is documented inline at lines 586-591 (schema_version bump for min_segment_length + removal of dead 'reference' field) and 595-602 (whiten=None mirrors the deferred-to-sorter reality where whitening happens inside Sorting._run_sorter, not at preprocessing).

Test coverage exists:
- tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:69-86 (`test_default_franklab_whiten_none`) asserts the preset name exists with whiten=None.
- tests/spikesorting/v2/_smoke_constants.py:141-146 provides explicit v1→v2 translation map `{"default": "default_franklab"}`, confirming the reviewer's point that the v1 'default' name no longer exists and migration is required.
- tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py uses 'default_franklab' throughout — heavy coverage of the new preset name.

The reviewer's claim that "no automatic migration path provided" is correct: `insert_default()` only inserts the three new presets via `skip_duplicates=True`; legacy rows named 'default' in an existing production database would not be renamed. Downstream Spyglass v1 code referencing `preproc_param_name='default'` would not find it in a fresh v2 install.

Classification new_in_v2 is appropriate (these are renamed/expanded presets, with documented rationale). Severity low is appropriate: rationale documented, tests present, name-translation map exists in test utilities, no scientific correctness impact within v2 itself (the values still correspond to reasonable bandpass settings). Operational migration concern is real but low-impact.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 `_validate_file` and `recompute`/`save_to` recompute-prior-to-deletion path missing in v2
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `_make_file(save_to=...)` writes a copy to a temp dir for hash-diff before delete, and `_validate_file` walks the H5 hierarchy to verify ProcessedElectricalSeries/electrodes are present (lines 269-404). Used by RecordingRecomputeSelection.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `_rebuild_nwb_artifact` rebuilds in the existing slot only (lines 1138-1180). No `save_to` parameter, no upstream-NWB-edit detection beyond a hash-mismatch warning. No `_validate_file`.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 has no RecordingRecompute machinery (per task context: 'no v2 equivalent; v2 keeps only the inline NwbfileHasher digest column'). v1's pre-delete validation is lost; v2 only logs a warning on hash mismatch.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Implicit per known v2 gaps; not individually documented in recording.py

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the reviewer's claim by reading both files directly.

v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:
- Line 269-367: `_make_file` signature includes `save_to: Union[str, Path] = None` (line 274). The `elif save_to:` branch at line 330-333 calls `cls._validate_file(file_path)` to verify the H5 hierarchy before allowing a recompute-prior-to-delete.
- Line 369-404: `_validate_file` walks `acquisition/ProcessedElectricalSeries/electrodes` H5 path and returns the electrodes object_id, raising FileNotFoundError or KeyError if any segment is missing.
- Line 673-681: `recompute` method itself raises NotImplementedError; the actual recompute orchestration lives in `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recompute.py` (RecordingRecompute table at line 527), which calls `_make_file(..., save_to=new.parent.parent, ...)` at line 717. That module also defines RecordingRecomputeVersions and RecordingRecomputeSelection.

v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:
- Lines 1138-1180: `_rebuild_nwb_artifact` rebuilds the artifact in the existing `analysis_file_name` slot. On hash mismatch it only logs a warning (lines 1171-1179): "The DataJoint row was not deleted; inspect upstream raw NWB / SI version before rerunning." No `save_to` parameter, no temp-dir copy, no `_validate_file` call.
- `grep _validate_file|save_to|RecordingRecompute` in v2/recording.py returns nothing.
- `ls v2/` shows no recompute.py module exists in v2 at all.

Tests: grep of tests/spikesorting/v2/ for `_rebuild_nwb_artifact` returns no hits — the v2 rebuild path is not directly tested. Only an unrelated hash-recomputation sanity check exists in test_single_session_pipeline.py:5605-5606.

The v2 docstring at lines 1138-1153 acknowledges the simplified design ("A mismatch surfaces silent upstream drift...the row is not auto-deleted so the user can diff before deciding"), so partial rationale exists, but the entire RecordingRecompute audit machinery (versions, selection, matched flag, environment tracking) is gone. Classification `missing_in_v2` is correct. Severity `medium` is appropriate: v2 preserves the row on mismatch (no data destruction), but the pre-delete validation and environment-versioned recompute audit trail are lost, and the rebuild path has no test coverage.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 ElectricalSeries 'filtering' / 'description' strings differ from v1
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `filtering='Bandpass filtered for spike band'`, `description='Referenced and filtered recording from {nwb_file_name} for spike sorting'`, table_region `description='Sort group'` (lines 840, 855-857).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: `filtering='Bandpass filter + common reference'`, `description='Pre-motion preprocessed recording from {nwb_file_name} for spike sorting'`, table_region `description='Sort group electrodes'` (lines 1897, 1904-1907).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Cosmetic/metadata change. NWB-conformance tooling or downstream consumers that pattern-match on these strings would break. No test asserts these strings.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale; appears to reflect the new bandpass+CMR stage semantics

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all three string divergences at the cited line numbers.

v1/recording.py:840-857 exactly contains:
- `description="Sort group"` (line 842)
- `filtering="Bandpass filtered for spike band"` (line 855)
- `description="Referenced and filtered recording from " + f"{nwb_file_name} for spike sorting"` (lines 856-857)

v2/recording.py:1895-1907 exactly contains:
- `description="Sort group electrodes"` (line 1897)
- `filtering="Bandpass filter + common reference"` (line 1904)
- `description=(f"Pre-motion preprocessed recording from " f"{nwb_file_name} for spike sorting")` (lines 1905-1907)

Tests verification: grep across tests/spikesorting/ for "Bandpass filter", "Pre-motion preprocessed", "Sort group electrodes", "Bandpass filtered for spike", "common reference" finds no assertion on these strings in either v1 or v2 test suites. The closest hit is a tangential mention of "common reference median" in a test docstring at test_parity_canonical.py:249, which does not assert the ElectricalSeries metadata.

Downstream usage: grep for the v1 strings across src/ finds them only in v1/recording.py itself — no internal code pattern-matches on them.

Rationale verification: read the v2 function docstring and surrounding comments (lines 1820-1915). The function does have detailed justification comments for one nearby behavioral change (heterogeneous-gain handling at lines 1853-1869), but the metadata-string changes at 1897/1904/1906 have no accompanying comment or docstring explanation. The new strings are arguably more accurate (Bandpass + CMR reflects the new preprocessing pipeline semantics; "Sort group electrodes" is more descriptive than "Sort group"), but this remains undocumented.

Classification: intentional_unjustified is correct — the strings track real semantic changes in the v2 preprocessing stack but no comment/docstring documents the choice.
Severity: low — pure metadata; no tests or internal consumers depend on the exact strings; external NWB-conformance pattern-matching is a theoretical concern with no concrete evidence in this codebase.

confirmed_findings.title: v2's invalid `sort_reference_electrode_id` error path lacks test coverage
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Raises ValueError on `ref_channel_id` not in {-1, -2, >=0} (lines 614-619). No v1 test invokes this path either.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Raises ValueError at `_apply_pre_motion_preprocessing` lines 1765-1770 on the same condition.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep for 'invalid sort_reference_electrode' / 'ref_channel_id.*invalid' / sort_reference_electrode_id values outside {-2,-1, valid_id} in tests/spikesorting/v2/ returns no matches — only -2 is exercised (test_single_session_pipeline.py:5285).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: n/a — error path port

verdict.reasoning:
Verified both cited code locations directly.

v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:614-619:
```
elif ref_channel_id != -1:
    raise ValueError(
        "Invalid reference channel ID. Use -1 to skip referencing. Use "
        + "-2 to reference via global median. Use positive integer to "
        + "reference to a specific channel."
    )
```

v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:1765-1770 (inside `_apply_pre_motion_preprocessing`):
```
elif ref_channel_id != -1:
    raise ValueError(
        "Recording.make: invalid sort_reference_electrode_id "
        f"{ref_channel_id}. Use -1 (no reference), -2 (global "
        "median), or a positive electrode id."
    )
```

Both implement the same guard semantics: after `ref_channel_id >= 0` and `ref_channel_id == -2`, anything else that isn't -1 (e.g., -3, -100) raises. The v2 message is more informative (includes the offending value) — minor improvement, not a behavior divergence.

Test coverage in tests/spikesorting/v2/:
- `grep -rn 'sort_reference_electrode_id'` shows only `-2` exercised (test_single_session_pipeline.py:5285) and the default `-1` documented at lines 5224-5227.
- No test sets `sort_reference_electrode_id` to a value like `-3` to trigger the ValueError.
- `grep` for the v2 error string ('invalid sort_reference') and v1 error string ('Invalid reference channel') in their respective test directories returns no matches — neither v1 nor v2 tests this branch.

Classification holds: untested error-path port. Severity is low because regression here would be at worst a missed/altered ValueError message — no silent numeric corruption.

confirmed_findings.title: v2's empty-intersection ValueError after min_segment_length filter has no test coverage
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `_consolidate_intervals` crashes with IndexError on `intervals[:, 0]` if `valid_times` ends up empty (recording.py:715-765). No graceful handling.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Explicit ValueError if `len(valid_times) == 0` after intersection + min_segment_length filter (lines 1424-1436) directing user to lower the threshold or fix IntervalList.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Grep across tests/spikesorting/v2/ for 'zero intersection' / 'min_segment_length filtering' shows no test forces this code path. A regression that swapped >= for > in min_length filtering would silently slip through.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: n/a — defensive guard

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all three claims:

1. **v1 behavior (recording.py:715-765)**: `_consolidate_intervals` does no empty-check on `intervals`. Lines 727-731 check `shape[1] != 2`, but if `valid_times` is `[]`, `np.array([])` has shape `(0,)`, which fails the shape check via IndexError (no `shape[1]`); even after `.reshape(-1, 2)` giving shape `(0, 2)`, line 747 `start, stop = start_indices[0], stop_indices[0]` raises IndexError on the empty array. v1/recording.py:557-560 calls `_consolidate_intervals(valid_sort_times, all_timestamps)` directly without an empty-check. So v1 does crash ungracefully.

2. **v2 behavior (recording.py:1420-1436)**: Confirmed verbatim — after `Interval(sort_valid_times).intersect(Interval(raw_valid_times), min_length=min_segment_length)` the code checks `if len(valid_times) == 0:` and raises a descriptive ValueError citing the interval_list_name, nwb_file_name, and min_segment_length, with a comment explicitly noting it prevents the downstream `_consolidate_intervals` crash. This matches the reviewer's claim exactly.

3. **Test coverage**: Grep across tests/spikesorting/v2/ for "min_segment_length", "zero intersection", "len(valid_times) == 0", "ValueError.*intersect", "empty.*valid_times" found:
   - test_single_session_pipeline.py:863 — tests a 2.5s window deliberately ABOVE the 1.0s threshold (positive case)
   - test_single_session_pipeline.py:1367 — comment says both segments "survive the min_segment_length filter" (positive case)
   - test_single_session_pipeline.py:4206 — empty `valid_times` discussion is in ArtifactDetection, not Recording.make
   - No test inserts an IntervalList where every chunk is shorter than min_segment_length to exercise the ValueError branch in Recording.make.

The divergence is real, the classification (untested_branch) is correct, and medium severity is reasonable — it's a defensive guard improvement over a crash, but a regression to the guard (e.g., changing `==` to `>` or deleting the check) would silently slip through CI.

confirmed_findings.title: v2's `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` in set_group_by_shank has no test coverage
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Behavior tested only indirectly via downstream pipeline tests in v1.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Code path at lines 313-335 with non-trivial mismatch from v1 (see related finding).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Grep `omit_ref_electrode_group` in tests/spikesorting/v2/ returns zero matches. Combined with the semantic drift from v1's per-shank reference lookup, this is a high-risk area.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: n/a

verdict.reasoning:
Verified the claim:

1. v2 code at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:313-335` exists exactly as the reviewer describes — a branch guarded by `if omit_ref_electrode_group:` that uses `sort_reference_electrode_id` (function-level kwarg, default -1) to identify which electrode group to skip:
```
if omit_ref_electrode_group:
    ref_match = electrodes["electrode_id"] == (
        sort_reference_electrode_id
    )
    if ref_match.any():
        ref_group = electrodes["electrode_group_name"][ref_match][0]
        if str(ref_group) == str(e_group):
            ... skipped.append({..., "reason": "reference_electrode_group"})
            continue
```

2. Test coverage confirmation: `grep -rn "omit_ref_electrode_group" tests/spikesorting/v2/` returns zero matches. Every test call to `SortGroupV2.set_group_by_shank(...)` (40+ call sites in test_single_session_pipeline.py, test_downstream_consumers.py, test_phase1_baseline_regen.py, baseline_capture.py) relies on the default `omit_ref_electrode_group=False`, so the branch at lines 313-335 is never executed.

3. The v2 semantic differs from v1 (v1's `get_group_by_shank` at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/utils.py:109` checks per-shank `original_reference_electrode` from the electrode table or a `references` dict, whereas v2 only checks against the function-wide `sort_reference_electrode_id`). This makes the lack of test coverage more concerning because the semantic drift is real and silent — but the claim under review is scoped to test coverage, which is confirmed.

Classification: untested_branch is correct. Severity medium is appropriate — the branch can produce different `skipped` outputs vs v1 (e.g., v1 would skip the e_group containing the original_reference_electrode from config; v2 only skips if `sort_reference_electrode_id` (default -1) happens to match a real electrode), but the default-False path means most pipelines avoid it. Users opting into `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` will silently get a different set of sort groups than v1.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 reference-channel removal uses `remove_channels` AFTER common_reference; v1 uses `channel_slice` AFTER common_reference
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: After `common_reference(reference='single', ref_channel_ids=ref_channel_id)`, does `recording.channel_slice(channel_ids=recording_channel_ids)` (lines 597-606).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: After `common_reference`, conditionally calls `recording.remove_channels([int(ref_channel_id)])` guarded by `if int(ref_channel_id) in [int(c) for c in recording.get_channel_ids()]` (lines 1745-1757).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 docstring at lines 1381-1385 notes SI 0.104 dropped `recording.channel_slice` method. `remove_channels` is the modern SI API. Defensive `if` is sound for v2's already-sliced input.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented SI API change

verdict.reasoning:
Verified both cited code locations exactly match the reviewer's claims.

v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:597-606:
```
if ref_channel_id >= 0:
    recording = si.preprocessing.common_reference(
        recording, reference="single", ref_channel_ids=ref_channel_id, dtype=np.float64,
    )
    recording = recording.channel_slice(channel_ids=recording_channel_ids)
```

v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:1745-1757:
```
if ref_channel_id >= 0:
    recording = sip.common_reference(
        recording, reference="single", ref_channel_ids=[int(ref_channel_id)], dtype=_np.float64,
    )
    if int(ref_channel_id) in [int(c) for c in recording.get_channel_ids()]:
        recording = recording.remove_channels([int(ref_channel_id)])
```

The v2 docstring at lines 1381-1385 explicitly documents the rationale: "Uses ChannelSliceRecording directly because SI 0.104 dropped the recording.channel_slice(...) method that was available on v1's SI 0.99 -- the constructor accepts the same kwargs." This is a documented SI API change.

The defensive `if int(ref_channel_id) in recording.get_channel_ids()` guard is consistent with the v2 docstring at lines 1376-1379 which notes the reference channel is included in a prior slice so common_reference can subtract it, then dropped after referencing.

Both paths achieve the same end state (reference channel removed from sorter's view of the recording). Classification of intentional_justified is correct.

Test coverage caveat: I confirmed via grep that the positive ref_channel_id branch (where remove_channels is invoked) appears untested in v2; only the -2 (global median) branch has explicit coverage at test_single_session_pipeline.py:5216+. The -1 path is what "every other recording test uses". However, this is a separate coverage gap, not a refutation of the divergence claim. The classification remains intentional_justified at low severity since the SI API change is documented and the end-state equivalence is plausible — though untested.

confirmed_findings.title: v1 single existing-row path uses delete-then-recreate in test mode; v2 doesn't have a test-mode early exit
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: `if existing_entries and test_mode: return` early-exits in test mode (line 82-83). Otherwise destroys and recreates.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: No test_mode handling. Always enforces inspect-before-destroy contract regardless of test_mode.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2's contract is uniform across environments — tests must explicitly invoke `delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=True` to reset state. This is the correctness-first philosophy.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Implicit in correctness-first goal

verdict.reasoning: Verified both file locations cited by reviewer. v1 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:81-86 reads: `existing_entries = SortGroup & {"nwb_file_name": nwb_file_name}; if existing_entries and test_mode: return; elif existing_entries: ... .delete()`. v1 also imports test_mode at line 22. v2 src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/recording.py:150-228 defines `_handle_existing` with NO reference to test_mode anywhere in the file (grep returned empty). v2 instead enforces a uniform contract requiring `delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=True` after reviewing `preview_existing_entries`, or an explicit non-overlapping `sort_group_ids` list. The docstring at lines 159-168 documents this: "Default rerun behavior is to REFUSE: callers must either pass explicit non-overlapping sort_group_ids (an opt-in additive insert) or set delete_existing_entries=True, confirm=True after reviewing the deletion preview." This is documented in-code as the inspect-before-destroy contract. Classification intentional_justified holds — the rationale is explicit in the docstring, not merely implicit in correctness-first philosophy. Severity low is appropriate because tests can still reset state with explicit flags; it only changes test-harness ergonomics, not scientific correctness.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 default amplitude threshold dropped from 3000 (v1) to 500 uV
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: ArtifactDetectionParameters 'default' row ships amplitude_thresh_uV=3000 (compared against RAW count traces in v1's _compute_artifact_chunk, since v1's NWB writer uses return_scaled=False so get_traces() returns ints, not uV).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: ArtifactDetectionParamsSchema default amplitude_thresh_uV=500.0 (compared against gain-scaled traces_uv = traces.astype(float32)*gains, i.e. actual microvolts).
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 schema doc at _params/artifact_detection.py:44-52 explicitly calls out the v1 unit-conversion bug: 'v1's 3000 nominal == ~585 µV on 0.195 µV/count Intan probes'. v2 fixes the bug by scaling to uV before comparison and lowers the numeric default to match v1's effective behavior. Tested in tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:42-56.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/_params/artifact_detection.py:44-52 and CHANGELOG; users with custom v1 thresholds must translate v1_value * probe_gain_uV_per_count to v2-equivalent uV.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all four citations directly:

1. v1 default = 3000: `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/artifact.py:70` contains `"amplitude_thresh_uV": 3000` in the 'default' contents row.

2. v1 raw-count comparison: `src/spyglass/spikesorting/utils.py:179` `above_a = np.abs(traces) > amplitude_thresh` — `traces` is the raw output of `recording.get_traces(...)` (line 171-175) with no gain scaling.

3. v1 writes raw ints: `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py:845` `recording=recording, return_scaled=False, buffer_gb=5` — confirms NWB writer stores int counts, so v1's 3000 threshold compares against counts not uV.

4. v2 default = 500.0: `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/_params/artifact_detection.py:54` `amplitude_thresh_uV: float | None = Field(default=500.0, ge=0.0)`.

5. v2 gain-scaled comparison: `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/artifact.py:870-874` reads `return_in_uV=False`, then `gains = recording.get_channel_gains(); traces_uv = traces.astype(_np.float32) * gains[None, :]; absolute = _np.abs(traces_uv)`; line 882-883 then compares `above_amp = absolute > validated.amplitude_thresh_uV` — clearly comparing in uV.

6. Rationale documented: `_params/artifact_detection.py:44-52` explicitly states "``amplitude_thresh_uV=500.0`` is v2's bug-fix default (matches v1's effective Intan-probe behavior within ~15%)" and references the CHANGELOG for the v1 unit-conversion bug. Migration guidance is given (`v1_value * probe_gain_uV_per_count`).

7. Test coverage exists: `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:42-56` `test_artifact_defaults_match_b1_revised` asserts `amplitude_thresh_uV == 500.0` and docstring repeats the rationale ("v1's 3000 nominal == ~585 µV on 0.195 µV/count Intan probes").

The divergence is real, documented as intentional (fixes a v1 unit-conversion bug), and tested. Severity is correctly high: silently treating 500 as uV vs. as counts changes which transients are flagged as artifacts and removed from valid_times, directly affecting which spikes the sorter sees. The classification of intentional_justified is correct.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 introduces explicit detect=bool field; v1 inferred from both-thresholds-None
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: No 'detect' field; _get_artifact_times treats `amplitude_thresh_uV is zscore_thresh is None` as the skip condition and returns the full window (v1/artifact.py:256-264).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Explicit 'detect: bool = True' field. ArtifactDetection._detect_artifacts short-circuits on `if not validated.detect`. The 'none' preset is detect=False with amplitude_thresh_uV=None.
confirmed_findings.evidence: v2 schema docstring (_params/artifact_detection.py:18-21) says the v2 'none' preset is 'a typed detect=False rather than an absent-fields blob'. _parity_canonical.py:289-301 documents the cross-version mapping. Validated in tests/spikesorting/v2/test_params_validation.py:88-91 (detect=False with no thresholds) and :96-99 (detect=True requires a threshold).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: v2 promotes the inferred semantics to an explicit boolean for schema typing; documented in v2 schema docstring.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all four cited lines.

v1/artifact.py:79-87 — 'none' preset contents confirmed: `{"zscore_thresh": None, "amplitude_thresh_uV": None, "chunk_duration": "10s", "n_jobs": 4, "progress_bar": "True"}`. No 'detect' key.
