v2 raise behavior (confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/spikesorting_merge.py:190-197):
```
allowed = set(rec_keys) | set(sort_keys) | set(curation_keys)
unknown = set(key) - allowed
if unknown:
    raise ValueError(
        "SpikeSortingOutput._get_restricted_merge_ids_v2: "
        f"unknown restriction keys {sorted(unknown)}. Allowed: "
        f"{sorted(allowed)}."
    )
```

v1 helper (lines 250-296) performs NO equivalent unknown-key check; it only does `table & key` style restrictions on join projections, so unknown keys are handled by DataJoint's restriction semantics (the v2 docstring at lines 153-156 asserts this drops silently).

Documented justification confirmed at lines 153-156:
"Unknown restriction keys raise ``ValueError`` rather than silently dropping through; v1 used to drop bad keys quietly, which made typos return wrong-but-non-empty results."

Cross-source asymmetry confirmed in dispatcher at lines 336-374: v1 branch (338-345) runs first and would return successfully on a typo'd key; v2 branch (361-372) then raises ValueError, aborting the whole call before merge_ids is returned. A user calling sources=["v1","v2"] with a typo gets ValueError even though the v1-only rows would have been returned.

Test coverage gap confirmed at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:364-371: the test calls `_get_restricted_merge_ids_v2` directly with `bogus_field`; there is no test that exercises `get_restricted_merge_ids(..., sources=["v1","v2"])` with an unknown key to assert the cross-source raise behavior.

Classification rationale: The raise is intentional and documented (the v2 author explicitly flagged the v1 silent-drop as a footgun). The cross-source asymmetry is a natural downstream consequence not separately documented, and is a real semantic change for mixed-source callers — but it's medium severity because (a) v1-only callers (the default `sources=["v0","v1"]` in pure-v1 deployments) are unaffected, (b) the raise is loud and immediate, and (c) the "wrong-but-non-empty result" failure mode it prevents is worse than the asymmetric raise.

One nuance: the docstring's claim that v1 "silently drops" unknown keys depends on DataJoint's restriction semantics on a join projection — in some configurations DataJoint can raise on unknown attributes. I trust the docstring's account as the author's motivation; the v2 raise behavior is the unambiguous fact.

confirmed_findings.title: CurationV2.insert_curation returns PK-only dict; CurationV1.insert_curation returns the full row
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: CurationV1.insert_curation at v1/curation.py:117-128 returns `{sorting_id, curation_id, parent_curation_id, analysis_file_name, object_id, merges_applied, description}` -- the full row dict.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: CurationV2.insert_curation at v2/curation.py:495 returns `{"sorting_id": ..., "curation_id": ...}` -- PK-only.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Direct read of both functions' return statements. v2's docstring at curation.py:216-218 explicitly says `Returns: dict {sorting_id, curation_id} PK-only dict.` No documented rationale for the change. v1's existing-row early-return at v1/curation.py:93 returns `parent_query.fetch("KEY")` -- already PK-only -- so v1 itself was inconsistent. Any downstream code doing `result["analysis_file_name"]` after calling insert_curation would KeyError on v2.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: no documented rationale; the docstring states the new contract but does not explain why it differs from v1.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified both return statements directly.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/curation.py:117-128) on the new-row path returns the full row dict:
```python
key = {
    "sorting_id": sorting_id,
    "curation_id": curation_id,
    "parent_curation_id": parent_curation_id,
    "analysis_file_name": analysis_file_name,
    "object_id": object_id,
    "merges_applied": apply_merge,
    "description": description,
}
cls.insert1(key, skip_duplicates=True)
return key
```

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/curation.py:495) returns PK-only:
```python
return {"sorting_id": sorting_id, "curation_id": curation_id}
```

v2's docstring at curation.py:215-218 explicitly documents the new contract ("`{"sorting_id": ..., "curation_id": ...}` PK-only dict") but does not justify why it diverges from v1.

v1 itself is internally inconsistent — its existing-row early return at v1/curation.py:93 (`return parent_query.fetch("KEY")`) returns a LIST of PK dicts, not the full row. So v1 already has two different return shapes depending on path. v2 is internally consistent (existing_root[0] at v2/curation.py:302 returns a single dict, same shape as the new-row path at line 495).

Downstream consumer audit:
- In-repo, only one caller of CurationV2.insert_curation exists: src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/pipeline.py:260. It uses only `curation_pk["curation_id"]` (line 276), so the PK-only contract suffices.
- v1's CurationV1.insert_metric_curation at v1/curation.py:152-161 returns the result of insert_curation upstream but never reads non-PK fields.
- The other v1 usages of `curation_key["analysis_file_name"]` and `curation_key["object_id"]` (v1/curation.py:246, 257; v1/figurl_curation.py:71, 80) all obtain `curation_key` via `(CurationV1 & key).fetch1()` — NOT from insert_curation's return. So no in-repo code breaks.

Severity is "low" — confirmed. Behavioral divergence is real and documented in v2's docstring, no in-repo consumer breaks, but downstream/user code that did `result["analysis_file_name"]` after calling v1 would KeyError on v2. Classification "intentional_unjustified" is correct: docstring states the new contract; no rationale given.

One nuance to the original finding: v1 is not uniformly "returns the full row" — its existing-row path already returned PK-only (as a list). So v2's PK-only return is arguably a normalization of v1's two-path inconsistency, just undocumented as such.

confirmed_findings.title: Sorting table `object_id` is varchar(72) in v2 vs varchar(40) in v1 SpikeSorting; both share the same NWB-object-key dict shape after `_get_nwb_object` substring trim, but inserting a v1 SpikeSorting analysis_file_name's object_id into v2 schema requires 72-char tolerance
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1/sorting.py:238 declares `object_id: varchar(40)` on SpikeSorting; v1/curation.py:38 declares `object_id: varchar(72)` on CurationV1.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2/sorting.py:426 declares `object_id: varchar(72)`; v2/curation.py:60 declares `object_id: varchar(72)`.
confirmed_findings.evidence: NWB object_ids are 36-char UUIDs in practice, so varchar(40) is sufficient for both. The widening to 72 is harmless and matches CurationV1's existing width. The `_get_nwb_object` dispatcher at utils/dj_helper_fn.py:430 keys on dict["object_id"] (after `.replace("_object_id", "")`) -- both v1 and v2 sorting use the literal `object_id` column name, so the downstream dict key is identical.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Implicit consistency with CurationV1's pre-existing varchar(72) width; not explicitly documented.

verdict.reasoning: Directly verified all four cited declarations: v1/sorting.py:238 (`object_id: varchar(40)`), v1/curation.py:38 (`object_id: varchar(72)`), v2/sorting.py:426 (`object_id: varchar(72)`), v2/curation.py:60 (`object_id: varchar(72)`). The `_get_nwb_object` dispatcher at utils/dj_helper_fn.py:429-435 keys on `id_attr.replace("_object_id", "")` — both v1 and v2 use the literal column name `object_id` (no prefix), so the dict key downstream is identical (`object_id`) and `_get_nwb_object` resolves via `nwbf.objects[object_id]` the same way. A codebase-wide grep confirms `varchar(40)` is the dominant convention for NWB object_id columns across Spyglass (LFP, common_ephys, common_behav, common_position, etc.) — because NWB object_ids are 36-char UUIDs in practice. The v2 widening to varchar(72) is harmless (any value that fits in 40 fits in 72) and consistent with the pre-existing CurationV1 width of 72. No behavioral divergence under any realistic NWB-generated object_id; no documented justification in comments/docstrings but the schema widening is benign. Classification `intentional_justified` and severity `low` are appropriate. One small caveat: a v1 SpikeSorting `object_id` (always a 36-char UUID) trivially fits the v2 width, so the finding's framing about "v2 requires 72-char tolerance" is technically true but practically vacuous — there's no realistic upstream UUID that exceeds 40 chars.

confirmed_findings.title: v2 only includes the CurationV2 part-table when v2 imports successfully -- conditional declaration ties FK availability to runtime import gate
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1's CurationV1 part on SpikeSortingOutput at spikesorting_merge.py:105-110 is ALWAYS declared.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2's CurationV2 part at spikesorting_merge.py:126-133 is conditional: `if CurationV2 is not None: class CurationV2(...)`. In v0/v1-only deployments or when the non-localhost-DB safety guard blocks the v2 import, the part is missing entirely.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Comments at spikesorting_merge.py:24-44 explicitly document: "v2 part table on SpikeSortingOutput is only declared when v2 is importable in the current environment, because DataJoint requires the FK target table to exist at schema-compile time." A consumer that does `SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV2 & ...` in a v0/v1-only env raises AttributeError; the test at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_legacy_runtime_boundary.py:182 covers `_default_merge_sources_skip_v2_when_unavailable` but does not cover the AttributeError path for downstream code that hard-codes the v2 part-table name.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented schema-compile-time FK constraint.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all claims by reading the actual code.

(1) v1 part-table unconditional declaration confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/spikesorting_merge.py:105-110:
```
class CurationV1(SpyglassMixin, dj.Part):  # noqa: F811
    definition = """
    -> master
    ---
    -> CurationV1
    """
```

(2) v2 part-table conditional declaration confirmed at spikesorting_merge.py:126-133:
```
if CurationV2 is not None:
    class CurationV2(SpyglassMixin, dj.Part):  # noqa: F811
        definition = """
        -> master
        ---
        -> CurationV2
        """
```

(3) Rationale documented explicitly in comments at spikesorting_merge.py:24-44, citing the DataJoint schema-compile-time FK constraint and the v2 DB-host safety guard. The try/except at lines 36-44 sets CurationV2 = None on import failure and stores the original exception in `_v2_import_error`.

(4) The codebase mitigates the AttributeError surface inside src/ by routing all v2-helper entry points through `_raise_v2_unavailable` (lines 47-68), which surfaces a clear RuntimeError including the original import exception. All `SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV2` references inside src/spyglass/ are within v2 modules (curation.py, sorting.py, pipeline.py) plus decoding/v1/waveform_features.py:188.

(5) Test coverage gap confirmed: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_legacy_runtime_boundary.py:182-198 only asserts `_available_merge_sources()` returns ["v0","v1"] when `CurationV2` is None. No test asserts the AttributeError raised by downstream code that hard-codes `SpikeSortingOutput.CurationV2` in a v0/v1-only env, nor does it verify decoding/v1/waveform_features.py degrades cleanly.

Classification intentional_justified at low severity is correct: the conditional declaration is forced by DataJoint's FK-target-must-exist-at-schema-compile-time invariant, is explicitly documented, and the v2-helper code paths all surface a clean RuntimeError via _raise_v2_unavailable. The remaining gap (no test for downstream AttributeError on v0/v1-only env) is real but minor.

confirmed_findings.title: DuplicateSelectionError replaces v1's silent warning + multi-row return
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: Multiple matching selection rows: v1 emits logger.warning("Similar row(s) already inserted.") and returns query.fetch(as_dict=True) -- a LIST of full row dicts.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2 raises DuplicateSelectionError (subclass of ValueError) if >1 rows match the logical-identity restriction; the find-existing branch returns exactly one PK-only dict, never a list.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Return-type shape change documented in v2/recording.py:697 ("never a list, never the full row"). v2/recording.py:684-686 explicitly justifies: "Raises ValueError if more than one matching row exists (which would only happen via direct dj.insert bypassing this helper -- it is an integrity bug, not user error)." Test coverage at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:5384 (pytest.raises(DuplicateSelectionError, match="duplicate")).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: v2 commits to UUID find-existing-or-insert semantics; multi-row case treated as structural bug not user warning. Documented in v2/recording.py:684-686 docstring.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all citations directly against the code.

v1 silent-warning + list-return pattern confirmed:
- v1/recording.py:176-179: `if query: logger.warning("Similar row(s) already inserted."); return query.fetch(as_dict=True)` — returns list of full row dicts.
- v1/artifact.py:123-126: identical pattern with `logger.warning`.
- v1/sorting.py:223-226: same shape but uses `logger.info`, NOT `logger.warning` as the finding states. Minor inaccuracy in the finding, but does not change the substantive divergence: v1 still silently logs + returns `query.fetch(as_dict=True)` (list of full rows).

v2 raise + single-PK-dict pattern confirmed:
- v2/exceptions.py:13: `class DuplicateSelectionError(ValueError)` — subclass relationship correct.
- v2/recording.py:705-713: returns `existing[0]` (single PK-only dict via `fetch("KEY", as_dict=True)`) on len==1; raises `DuplicateSelectionError(f"...{len(existing)} duplicate selection rows...")` on len>1.
- v2/recording.py:697-698 docstring: `"never a list, never the full row"` — return-type contract documented.
- v2/recording.py:684-686 docstring: `"Raises ValueError if more than one matching row exists (which would only happen via direct dj.insert bypassing this helper -- it is an integrity bug, not user error)."` — rationale documented verbatim.
- v2/artifact.py:468-475 and v2/sorting.py:312-319: same raise-on-duplicate pattern (on the deduped unique-master-PK set after joining master * source part).

Test coverage confirmed at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:5322-5395 — `test_recording_selection_raises_on_duplicate_logical_identity` plants two rows via raw `insert1` to bypass the helper, then asserts `pytest.raises(DuplicateSelectionError, match="duplicate")`.

Classification `intentional_justified` holds: docstring explicitly frames multi-row as a structural integrity bug (not user error), return-type shape change is documented, and dedicated test pins the new contract. Severity `high` is reasonable because any caller relying on the v1 list-return shape will break under v2 (TypeError on indexing or iteration), and exception type is now ValueError not a silent warning.

confirmed_findings.title: SchemaBypassError is a new structural check with no v1 precedent
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 uses single-FK Selection rows (no source-part pattern); the failure mode (master with 0 or 2 source-part rows) is structurally impossible in v1 and the exception class does not exist.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: resolve_source() (called from make()) fetches both source parts and raises SchemaBypassError if total != 1. Message names the table, PK, and points the caller at insert_selection().
confirmed_findings.v1_location: n/a -- v1 has no source-part schema (v1/sorting.py:198-206, v1/artifact.py:97-105 show single-FK selection tables).
confirmed_findings.evidence: Source-part pattern is v2-only (v2/artifact.py:151+ adds SharedArtifactGroupSource; v2/sorting.py adds RecordingSource xor ConcatenatedRecordingSource). The defensive except SchemaBypassError in artifact.py:1099 explicitly comments "the only thing resolve_source raises." Test at test_single_session_pipeline.py:2401 (pytest.raises(SchemaBypassError, match="0 source part")).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in exceptions.py:19-22 as source-part integrity invariant; consumer-side recovery in artifact.py:1099-1113 documents why a broad except would silently orphan IntervalList rows.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all claims by direct code inspection.

(1) v1 has no exceptions.py and no SchemaBypassError class — `grep -rn "SchemaBypassError" v1/` returns empty; v1 directory listing has no exceptions.py.

(2) v1 selection tables use a single FK to SpikeSortingRecording, making the source-part divergence structurally impossible:
- v1/sorting.py:198-206: `SpikeSortingSelection` definition with `-> SpikeSortingRecording` (single FK).
- v1/artifact.py:97-105: `ArtifactDetectionSelection` definition with `-> SpikeSortingRecording` (single FK).

(3) v2 introduces source-part subtables:
- v2/sorting.py:202-229: `SortingSelection` with `RecordingSource` and `ConcatenatedRecordingSource` part tables.
- v2/artifact.py:364-388: `ArtifactSelection` with `RecordingSource` and `SharedArtifactGroupSource` part tables.

(4) `SchemaBypassError` is defined as cited at v2/exceptions.py:19-22 with docstring documenting the invariant ("zero or multiple source part rows at populate time", "expected exactly one source", "points the caller at insert_selection()").

(5) `resolve_source()` raises exactly as described:
- v2/sorting.py:383-397: imports SchemaBypassError, computes `total = len(rec_rows) + len(concat_rows)`, raises if `total != 1` with the documented message.
- v2/artifact.py:551-565: analogous, using `rec_rows + shared_rows`.

(6) Defensive consumer at v2/artifact.py:1099-1114 explicitly catches ONLY SchemaBypassError with a comment "A genuinely broken source (zero/multiple source part rows -- the only thing resolve_source raises) cannot be resolved... DO NOT swallow it (a broad except would silently orphan IntervalList rows)." This matches the finding's rationale verbatim.

(7) Test coverage exists at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:2401: `with pytest.raises(SchemaBypassError, match="0 source part"):`, inserting an orphan master with no source part to simulate bypass.

Classification new_in_v2 is correct: the failure mode does not exist in v1 because the source-part schema is v2-only. Severity medium is appropriate — it's a defensive structural guard (not a correctness regression), it has test coverage, and it has documented invariant + recovery path in the consumer. The finding's premise (that this is a new structural check with no v1 precedent) is fully supported by code.

confirmed_findings.title: RecordingTruncatedError adds a save-coverage guard that does not exist in v1
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 SpikeSortingRecording writes the preprocessed ElectricalSeries to AnalysisNwbfile and inserts without comparing requested vs saved duration. No truncation check (grep "truncat" v1/recording.py finds only an unrelated KeyError at line 401).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2 Recording.make_insert computes requested_total = sum(end - start for start, end in sort_valid_times), compares against saved_total with tolerance = 1.5 / sampling_frequency, and on excess deletes the orphan analysis NWB file before raising RecordingTruncatedError.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Multi-test coverage at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:1238, 1297, 1338, 1402, 1567 (positive case match="Missing: \d" plus a negative no-spurious-raise case at 1567). Cleanup-before-raise (unlink orphan AnalysisNwbfile) documented at recording.py:1049-1058.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in exceptions.py:25-30. v2 NwbfileHasher replaces the v1 RecordingRecompute pipeline; this guard is the new in-pipeline integrity check.

verdict.reasoning:
All claims verified directly against the code.

1) v1 has no truncation guard. grep for "truncat" in src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/recording.py returns no hits. v1 SpikeSortingRecording.make_insert (lines 241-260) does only:
   - IntervalList.insert1(sort_interval_valid_times.as_dict, skip_duplicates=True)
   - AnalysisNwbfile().add(nwb_file_name, insert_key["analysis_file_name"])
   - self.insert1(insert_key); self._record_environment(insert_key)
No duration comparison, no tolerance, no raise.

2) v2 guard exists exactly as described. recording.py:1027-1068:
   - imports `from spyglass.spikesorting.v2.exceptions import RecordingTruncatedError`
   - `requested_total = float(sum(end - start for start, end in sort_valid_times))` (1042-1044)
   - `saved_total = float(saved_end - saved_start)` (1045)
   - `tolerance = 1.5 / sampling_frequency` (1046)
   - `if missing > tolerance:` then `_pathlib.Path(abs_path).unlink(missing_ok=True)` (1054) and `raise RecordingTruncatedError(...)` (1060) with message containing "Missing: {missing:.6f}s" (1066).

3) Exception class is defined at v2/exceptions.py:25-30 with the documented docstring naming requested interval, saved interval, and missing seconds.

4) Test coverage matches the cited lines:
   - tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:1238 imports RecordingTruncatedError
   - :1297 `pytest.raises(RecordingTruncatedError, match="Missing: .* seconds|Missing: \\d")`
   - :1338 second import for second test
   - :1402 `pytest.raises(RecordingTruncatedError, match="Missing: \\d")`
   - :1567 negative case comment "no spurious RecordingTruncatedError"

5) Justification holds: v1 records environment via `rcp.RecordingRecomputeSelection().insert(...)` at v1/recording.py:267 — a post-hoc recompute-based integrity mechanism. v2 replaces this with an in-pipeline truncation guard at write time, consistent with the "NwbfileHasher replaces RecordingRecompute pipeline" narrative. The 1.5/fs tolerance is explicitly justified in the docstring (1022-1023) as covering the NWB `last_ts = (N-1)/fs` off-by-one. The disjoint-interval rationale (1035-1041) explains why summed chunk durations are used instead of the outer envelope — without this, multi-interval (R5 disjoint) sorts would spuriously trip the guard.

Classification: new_in_v2 (v1 has no equivalent path; this is additive, not divergent behavior). Severity: high is appropriate because (a) silently writing a shorter recording than requested would corrupt all downstream sorting/decoding, and (b) the guard ships with both positive and negative tests so the contract is encoded.

confirmed_findings.title: NonIntegerUnitIDError fires where v1 would silently pass through to pynwb
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 _write_sorting_to_nwb_with_curation does `for unit_id in sorting.get_unit_ids(): nwbf.add_unit(... id=unit_id ...)` with no int coercion. Non-integer unit_id would be stored as-is or surface a confusing pynwb/HDF5 error downstream.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2 _populate_unit_part wraps int(unit_id) in try/except and raises NonIntegerUnitIDError("... v2's Sorting.Unit stores int unit_ids; remap before insertion if the sorter emits non-convertible IDs.") if conversion fails.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep "NonIntegerUnitIDError\|non_integer\|nonint" tests/spikesorting/v2/ returns zero hits. Exception has exactly one raise site (sorting.py:1613) and zero test files: NonIntegerUnitIDError: raises=1 test_files=0.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in exceptions.py:33-36 and at the raise site (sorting.py:1614-1618: "v2's Sorting.Unit stores int unit_ids; remap before insertion"). No test exercises the branch.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified directly against source.

v1 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/sorting.py:592-599): `for unit_id in sorting.get_unit_ids(): ... nwbf.add_unit(spike_times=..., id=unit_id, obs_intervals=obs_interval, curation_label="uncurated")` — no int coercion, no try/except. A non-int unit_id would flow straight into pynwb.

v2 (src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/sorting.py:1609-1618):
```
for unit_id in sorting.unit_ids:
    try:
        int_unit_id = int(unit_id)
    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
        raise NonIntegerUnitIDError(
            f"Sorting.make: sorter returned unit_id {unit_id!r} "
            "that does not convert to int. v2's Sorting.Unit "
            "stores int unit_ids; remap before insertion if "
            "the sorter emits non-convertible IDs."
        ) from exc
```
The exception class is defined at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/exceptions.py:33-36 with a docstring describing the intent.

Test coverage check: `grep -rn "NonIntegerUnitIDError\|non_integer\|nonint" tests/spikesorting/v2/` returns zero matches. The raise site is the only one in v2 source (sorting.py:1613), imported once at sorting.py:1578.

The divergence is intentional (matches schema-first design where Sorting.Unit.unit_id is typed int) and documented at both the exception class and raise site, but the branch is untested. Classification `untested_branch` and severity `medium` are both supported.

confirmed_findings.title: SessionGroupDateError is declared but never raised and never tested
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 has no SessionGroup concept at all; cross-session grouping is not a v1 feature.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Exception is defined with a detailed docstring describing SessionGroup.create_group multi-day enforcement. v2/session_group.py:84-86 and :94-96 (create_group and is_multi_day) raise plain NotImplementedError instead -- the documented exception is never wired up.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep -rn "SessionGroupDateError" in src/ and tests/ finds the definition only -- zero raise sites and zero tests: SessionGroupDateError: raises=0 test_files=0. Consistent with the schema-first forward-declaration pattern at session_group.py:1-14.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Forward-declared per schema-first / zero-migration policy (session_group.py:1-14 docstring). No documented justification for shipping the exception class ahead of its raise site.

verdict.reasoning: Verified directly. `grep -rn "SessionGroupDateError"` over src/ and tests/ returns only the class definition at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/exceptions.py:39` — zero `raise` sites, zero test references. The class is declared (lines 39-45) with a docstring promising it is raised by `SessionGroup.create_group()` for caller-supplied `recording_date` or for members spanning multiple dates without `allow_multi_day=True`. But `session_group.py:84-86` (`create_group`) and `session_group.py:94-96` (`is_multi_day`) both raise plain `NotImplementedError("... is not yet implemented")`, and `create_group`'s `allow_multi_day` parameter (line 76) is never even read. The schema-first/zero-migration docstring at `session_group.py:1-14` explicitly justifies forward-declaring the *tables* so `SortingSelection` can FK them from day one ("schema is frozen; the populate body... is gated behind NotImplementedError until the concat materializer lands"), but it does not specifically justify shipping the exception class itself ahead of its raise site. v1 has no SessionGroup module — this is a new v2-only surface, so there is no v1 behavior to diverge from. Classification `untested_branch` fits: the branch that would raise the exception does not exist yet, so it is structurally untestable rather than tested-and-broken. Severity `low` is right because the exception is dead code today (cannot be triggered) and the only real risk is that whoever wires up `create_group` later forgets to use this specific subclass instead of a bare `ValueError`; the docstring at lines 40-45 serves as the contract reminder.

confirmed_findings.title: MissingRecordingForConcatError is declared but never raised and never tested
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 has no concat-recording pipeline; the failure mode does not exist in v1.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Exception defined in v2/exceptions.py:58-63 with a docstring naming ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection() and ConcatenatedRecording.make() as raisers. Both methods (session_group.py:171-181, 215-225) raise NotImplementedError instead; no per-member Recording check exists in current code.
confirmed_findings.v1_location: n/a -- v1 has no concat pipeline.
confirmed_findings.evidence: MissingRecordingForConcatError: raises=0 test_files=0. Same forward-declaration pattern as SessionGroupDateError; the schema-first policy declares the exception ahead of consumer logic.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Forward-declared per schema-first / zero-migration policy (session_group.py:1-14).

verdict.reasoning:
Verified directly:

1. exceptions.py:58-63 defines `MissingRecordingForConcatError(RuntimeError)` with docstring: "`ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection()` or `ConcatenatedRecording.make()` cannot find a populated per-member `Recording` row with the shared `preproc_params_name`. Message lists the missing member keys and instructs the caller to populate `Recording` for those members first."

2. session_group.py:178-181: `ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection` raises `NotImplementedError("ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection is not yet implemented")` — does NOT raise `MissingRecordingForConcatError`.

3. session_group.py:223-225: `ConcatenatedRecording.make` raises `NotImplementedError("ConcatenatedRecording.make() is not implemented yet")` — does NOT raise `MissingRecordingForConcatError`. No per-member Recording check exists.

4. `grep -rn "MissingRecordingForConcatError" src tests` returns exactly one hit: the class definition itself. Zero raises, zero test references.

5. session_group.py:1-14 documents the schema-first / zero-migration policy: "The schema is frozen; the populate body of `ConcatenatedRecording` is gated behind `NotImplementedError` until the concat materializer lands -- only the consumer logic changes, never the row shape." This is consistent with forward-declaring the matching exception class.

6. v1 has no concat pipeline, so there is no v1 equivalent to diverge from — this is new_in_v2 surface area, but currently untested because the consumer is still a stub.

Classification "untested_branch" fits: the exception exists and is documented as part of the planned API, but no code path currently raises it and no test exercises it. Severity "low" is appropriate — no runtime regression vs v1 (v1 has no equivalent), and the NotImplementedError stubs prevent silent misuse.

confirmed_findings.title: ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError has two raise sites but zero test coverage
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 has no get_unit_brain_regions analogue at the Sorting/Curation level (grep get_unit_brain_regions v1/*.py returns nothing); the failure mode and exception class do not exist.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Sorting.get_unit_brain_regions and CurationV2.get_unit_brain_regions raise ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError when resolve_source(...).kind == "concatenated_recording" and allow_anchor_member=False. The branch is reachable only via a concat source-part row -- which itself is gated upstream by NotImplementedError in the concat materializer.
confirmed_findings.v1_location: n/a -- no v1 brain-region resolution at the sort level.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep -rn "ConcatBrainRegion\|allow_anchor_member" tests/spikesorting/v2/ finds zero matches. Single-session path IS tested at test_single_session_pipeline.py:2057-2073, but neither raise site is covered. Count: ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError: raises=2 test_files=0.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in exceptions.py:48-55 ("per-session regions require cross-session unit matching, not available in this build"). The branch requires a concat-source fixture that the upstream materializer cannot produce yet, so test coverage will arrive with the concat consumer.

verdict.reasoning:
All claims verified against the code:

(1) Two raise sites confirmed: 
- src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/sorting.py:977-985 raises ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError inside Sorting.get_unit_brain_regions when `source.kind == "concatenated_recording"` and `allow_anchor_member=False`.
- src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/curation.py:1137-1143 raises the same exception in CurationV2.get_unit_brain_regions under identical conditions.

(2) Zero test coverage confirmed: `grep -rn "ConcatBrainRegion\|allow_anchor_member" tests/spikesorting/` returns no matches.

(3) v1 has no analogue: `grep -rn "get_unit_brain_regions" src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` returns no matches.

(4) Single-session branch IS exercised: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:2062 calls `SpikeSortingOutput.get_unit_brain_regions({"merge_id": merge_id})` which dispatches through CurationV2.get_unit_brain_regions, hitting the `else: resolution = "single_session"` branch (curation.py:1145-1146) — but the concat branch is never reached.

(5) Upstream gate confirmed: session_group.py:178 (`ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection` raises NotImplementedError) and session_group.py:223 (`ConcatenatedRecording.make()` raises NotImplementedError) make it impossible to create a `kind == "concatenated_recording"` source row through the public API today. sorting.py:472 also rejects `Sorting.make` on concat sources with NotImplementedError. So the raise site is genuinely unreachable via supported flow.

(6) Documented justification: exceptions.py:48-55 explicitly states "per-session regions require cross-session unit matching, not available in this build" — the guard is forward-declared by design.

Severity medium is appropriate: it's a forward-compat guard with documented rationale, currently unreachable, but will need test coverage when the concat materializer lands. Not low (two distinct raise sites for a non-trivial user-facing error path), not high (no reachable runtime bug today).

confirmed_findings.title: PipelineInputError docstring overstates what is currently validated
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 has no run_v2_pipeline orchestrator; the failure mode is v2-specific.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: Docstring (exceptions.py:66-69) promises: "run_v2_pipeline() receives zero, partial, or mixed input modes. Message says exactly one input mode is required and lists the required fields for each mode." Actual run_v2_pipeline (pipeline.py:181-187) only validates `preset not in _PRESETS` and raises with an "unknown preset" message; there is no input-mode XOR check.
confirmed_findings.evidence: run_v2_pipeline has a fixed signature with no alternative input modes; the exception docstring describes a forward-looking design (multi-mode entry points) that the current code does not implement. Only the preset check uses the exception. Test at test_single_session_pipeline.py:2367 covers the preset path (match="unknown preset").
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: No documented justification for the docstring/behavior mismatch. Likely a placeholder for a future multi-input-mode signature (concat / session-group inputs); should be either narrowed to match current behavior or implemented.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified directly against source.

1. exceptions.py:66-69 docstring claims PipelineInputError fires when "run_v2_pipeline() receives zero, partial, or mixed input modes. Message says exactly one input mode is required and lists the required fields for each mode."

2. pipeline.py:86-94 shows run_v2_pipeline has a fixed signature with no alternative input modes:
   def run_v2_pipeline(nwb_file_name: str, sort_group_id: int, interval_list_name: str, team_name: str, preset: str = "franklab_tetrode_mountainsort5", description: str = "", require_units: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]
   All four data-identifying parameters are required positional/keyword; there is no XOR-style entry point.

3. pipeline.py:181-187 is the ONLY raise PipelineInputError site in the entire codebase, and the message is "run_v2_pipeline: unknown preset {preset!r}. Available presets: ..." — not the docstring's input-mode message.

4. grep across src/ and tests/ confirms PipelineInputError has exactly one definition, one raise, and one test (test_single_session_pipeline.py:2367) which matches on "unknown preset". No XOR / multi-mode validation exists anywhere.

5. grep confirms run_v2_pipeline is the only run_*/`*_pipeline` function in v2 — there is no sibling concat/session-group entry point that would justify the multi-mode docstring.

Classification intentional_unjustified holds: the docstring describes a forward-looking multi-input-mode design (likely concat / session-group orchestrator) that has not been implemented; the exception is currently repurposed for a single unknown-preset path. Severity low: docstring/behavior mismatch only, no scientific or operational impact today.

confirmed_findings.title: ZeroUnitSortError converts v1's silent empty-units NWB into an opt-in hard error
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 SpikeSorting silently writes an empty pynwb.misc.Units table (nwbf.units = pynwb.misc.Units(name="units", description="Empty units table.")) when sorting.get_num_units() == 0 -- no warning, no error, no caller-facing signal.
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2 Sorting.make also writes an empty sort row (graceful by default); run_v2_pipeline only raises ZeroUnitSortError if the caller opted in with require_units=True (pipeline.py:228-235). Otherwise it logs a warning and proceeds to insert an empty curation+merge row to keep the result merge-keyable.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Exception docstring at exceptions.py:73-79 explicitly: "Zero units is a legitimate result on a quiet shank, so it is graceful by default ... raised only when the caller requires units." Pipeline comment at pipeline.py:222-224 reiterates the v1-parity rationale. Test at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3495 (pytest.raises(ZeroUnitSortError)).
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in exceptions.py:72-79 and pipeline.py:222-247 ("Fall through ... matching v1, which writes an empty Units table"). Default behavior preserved; opt-in raise is the only new branch.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified all citations against the actual code.

v1 silent empty-Units write confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/sorting.py:578-581:
```
if sorting.get_num_units() == 0:
    nwbf.units = pynwb.misc.Units(
        name="units", description="Empty units table."
    )
```
No warning, no error, no caller-facing signal — silent.

v2 opt-in raise confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/pipeline.py:226-235:
```
n_units = int((Sorting & sort_pk).fetch1("n_units"))
if n_units == 0:
    recording_id = rec_pk["recording_id"]
    if require_units:
        raise ZeroUnitSortError(...)
```
Followed by fall-through warning at pipeline.py:242-247 ("writing an EMPTY curation + merge row").

Rationale explicit at pipeline.py:222-224: "Zero units is a legitimate result on a quiet shank. Unless the caller set require_units=True, proceed to build an empty (but real) curation + merge row so the result is merge-keyable like any other." And at pipeline.py:236-241: "Fall through to the normal curation + merge insert ... matching v1, which writes an empty Units table."

Exception docstring confirmed verbatim at exceptions.py:72-79: "Zero units is a legitimate result on a quiet shank, so it is graceful by default (partial manifest with curation_id/merge_id None plus a warning); this is raised only when the caller requires units."

Test coverage confirmed at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3495: `with pytest.raises(ZeroUnitSortError): run_v2_pipeline(..., require_units=True)`.

Additional confirmation: v2 Sorting.make also handles zero units gracefully at sorting.py:1396-1403 (skips analyzer build with a warning, commits master row with n_units=0), matching the "graceful by default" stance. This is a parallel branch — the empty-sort handling is layered at both Sorting.make (skip analyzer) and run_v2_pipeline (warn + empty merge row, or opt-in raise).

The classification intentional_justified holds: default behavior matches v1 (silent-ish empty result, modulo a warning vs zero output), and the only new caller-facing branch is opt-in via require_units. Medium severity is appropriate — new API surface (require_units kwarg) and a new warning, but no default-path regression.

confirmed_findings.title: ZeroUnitAnalyzerError fires where v1's analyzer concept does not exist
confirmed_findings.v1_behavior: v1 has no SortingAnalyzer concept (uses the legacy SpikeInterface waveform extractor path); no equivalent get_analyzer method on v1 SpikeSorting (grep get_analyzer v1/sorting.py returns nothing).
confirmed_findings.v2_behavior: v2 Sorting.get_analyzer checks int((self & key).fetch1("n_units")) == 0 and raises ZeroUnitAnalyzerError instead of attempting to load a never-written analyzer folder (SI cannot build a SortingAnalyzer over zero units -- estimate_sparsity crashes on np.concatenate([])). get_sorting() still returns an empty NumpySorting for callers that only need the unit list.
confirmed_findings.v2_location: src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/sorting.py:841-848 (raise ZeroUnitAnalyzerError); the parallel get_sorting zero-unit handler at sorting.py:749-757 returns empty NumpySorting and does NOT raise.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Docstring at sorting.py:827-832 and exception docstring at exceptions.py:82-89 reference SI internals (estimate_sparsity / np.concatenate([])). Test at tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3513 exercises the raise. Contrast with non-raising get_sorting() at sorting.py:749-757 shows the choice is intentional and scoped.
confirmed_findings.rationale_if_known: Documented in both the raise-site comment (sorting.py:827-832) and exceptions.py:82-89: loading a phantom folder would surface a confusing SI error -- this is the clean signal.

verdict.reasoning:
Verified by direct code inspection.

1. v1 has no SortingAnalyzer surface: `grep -rn "SortingAnalyzer\|analyzer\|get_analyzer" src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` returns zero matches. v1's `Sorting.get_sorting` at v1/sorting.py:499 is the only retrieval API. The claim "v1 has no analyzer concept" holds.

2. v2 raise site confirmed at src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/sorting.py:841-848:
```
if int((self & key).fetch1("n_units")) == 0:
    raise ZeroUnitAnalyzerError(
        "Sorting.get_analyzer: sorting_id="
        f"{key['sorting_id']!r} has zero units; no "
        "SortingAnalyzer exists (SI cannot build one over zero "
        "units). Use get_sorting() if you only need the empty "
        "unit list, or re-sort with a lower detect_threshold."
    )
```

3. Docstring at sorting.py:827-832 explicitly justifies the raise: "SI cannot build a SortingAnalyzer over zero units, so no folder was written by _build_analyzer ... Loading it would surface a confusing SI/file error; this raises a clear signal instead."

4. Parallel `get_sorting` zero-unit handler confirmed at sorting.py:749-765 — returns empty NumpySorting and explicitly cross-references the asymmetric choice in the comment ("get_analyzer on a zero-unit sort raises instead"). The asymmetry is intentional and self-documented.

5. Exception docstring at exceptions.py:82-89 names the SI internal (`estimate_sparsity` -> `np.concatenate([])`) — matches finding.

6. Test coverage confirmed: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:3513 uses `pytest.raises(ZeroUnitAnalyzerError)`, and the happy path at :1204 covers `get_analyzer` loading a populated folder.

This is a v2-only API surface that has no v1 counterpart, so it is not "drift from v1." It is a documented, tested, intentional design choice with clear rationale (clean signal instead of a confusing SI error from a phantom analyzer folder). Severity assessment of "medium" is slightly generous since this is not a behavioral regression — v1 callers had no analyzer to call — but the classification of `intentional_justified` is correct.

refuted_findings.title: v2 parse_artifact_interval_list_name helper exists but is never called
refuted_findings.why_refuted:
The reviewer's claim that `parse_artifact_interval_list_name` has "zero call sites in src or tests" is REFUTED. The function IS actively used by the merge-table dispatcher at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/spikesorting_merge.py:199-214`:

```
from spyglass.spikesorting.v2.utils import (
    parse_artifact_interval_list_name,
)
...
if restrict_by_artifact and "interval_list_name" in key:
    artifact_id = parse_artifact_interval_list_name(
        key["interval_list_name"]
    )
    if artifact_id is not None:
        key["artifact_id"] = artifact_id
        key.pop("interval_list_name", None)
```

This is exactly the "merge-dispatcher's leave-non-artifact-names-alone contract" the docstring at utils.py:526-527 refers to: when the interval name isn't of the `artifact_{uuid}` form, the helper returns `None` and the caller leaves the key alone (rather than corrupting it).

The reviewer's grep was scoped to `/cumulus/edeno/spyglass/src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/` and `/cumulus/edeno/spyglass/tests/spikesorting/v2/`, missing the consumer one directory up in the parent `spikesorting/` package where the merge table lives.

The consuming code path `_get_restricted_merge_ids_v2(..., restrict_by_artifact=True)` has test coverage in at least:
- `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_legacy_runtime_boundary.py:202-214` (specifically tests the non-artifact-name branch)
- `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:6105-6138` (`test_merge_dispatch_restrict_by_artifact_honored_in_v2`)
- `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_v1_parity.py:369`
- `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:2249` (`test_get_restricted_merge_ids_v2_resolves_through_chain`)

So both branches of `parse_artifact_interval_list_name` (artifact-prefixed → return id; not prefixed → return None) are exercised. Not dead code, not pending integration, and not an untested branch.

refuted_findings.title: MotionCorrectionParameters.job_kwargs field is wired up but completely undocumented and unused
refuted_findings.why_refuted:
The reviewer's factual claims about the code are accurate: `job_kwargs=null: blob` exists on line 115 of `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/session_group.py`, defaults pass `None` at lines 123/129/135, and `MotionCorrectionParamsSchema` (in `_params/motion_correction.py`) does not include a `job_kwargs` field. The consumer `ConcatenatedRecording.make()` is gated with `NotImplementedError` (session_group.py:224). However, the classification "intentional_unjustified" is wrong because the rationale IS documented, as a system-wide convention rather than per-table:

1. `_params/sorter.py:22-26` explicitly documents the convention: *"Concurrency parameters (n_jobs, chunk_duration, progress_bar) do NOT live on these schemas. They are stored on the per-row job_kwargs blob column and resolved by _resolved_job_kwargs at populate time per the shared-contracts Job-Kwargs Resolution convention."*

2. `_params/artifact_detection.py:31-33` repeats the same documentation: *"Concurrency parameters ... are not part of this schema. They live in the per-row job_kwargs blob and are resolved at populate time by _resolved_job_kwargs."*

3. The same `job_kwargs=null: blob` column appears in `artifact.py:109`, `recording.py:583`, `sorting.py:94`, and `session_group.py:115` — establishing the pattern across all v2 params Lookups.

4. The merge/resolution logic is implemented and tested: `utils.py:_resolved_job_kwargs` (line 297) layers SI globals + `dj.config['custom']['spikesorting_v2_job_kwargs']` + per-row overrides; `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_scaffold.py:69-93` (`test_resolved_job_kwargs_merge`) verifies the precedence rules.

5. The session_group.py class docstring (lines 102-108) explicitly justifies the schema-first / pre-consumer landing: *"Ships before the consumer (ConcatenatedRecording.make()) lands so this Lookup's insert1 can Pydantic-validate its params blob at insert time ... the consumer activates without a migration once the concat materializer is implemented."*

Omitting `job_kwargs` here would break the v2 convention and require a schema migration when the concat consumer lands. The column's presence with `null` defaults is consistent with the other v2 params tables and is the deliberate zero-migration policy. The finding is best classified as intentional_justified, severity n/a — there is no real divergence or bug, and the reviewer's claim of "no documented rationale" overlooks the shared-contracts convention spelled out in the sibling _params files.

refuted_findings.title: v2 interval_list_name argument is passed through unchanged; v1 populator could auto-create a SortInterval from the IntervalList
refuted_findings.why_refuted:
The reviewer's citation is mis-attributed. They cite v1 behavior at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v0/spikesorting_populator.py:171-198` — but that is the v0 module, not v1. The actual v1 code at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` has no populator and no SortInterval table.

v0 (cited as "v1"): `v0/spikesorting_populator.py:184` does pull `valid_times[0]` and `:189-196` inserts a SortInterval row when `sort_interval_name is None`. That matches the reviewer's description of v0.

Real v1: `v1/recording.py:208-217` consumes `interval_list_name` directly via `RecordingSelection` / `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection`. There is no SortInterval table in v1 at all — `grep -rn SortInterval src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` returns no hits. v1 already collapsed the v0 SortInterval indirection.

v2 (`v2/pipeline.py:193-201`) passes `interval_list_name` directly to `RecordingSelection.insert_selection` — identical to the v1 design.

So this is not a v1→v2 divergence. v2 preserves the v1 contract. The "intentional schema collapse from SortInterval → IntervalList" actually happened in the v0→v1 transition, not in v2. The finding is refuted as a v1↔v2 parity issue.

refuted_findings.title: v2 has no equivalent of v1's SpikeSortingPipelineParameters lookup table
refuted_findings.why_refuted:
The claim conflates v0 with v1, mis-states the rationale, and ignores existing test coverage.

1. Wrong baseline (v0 vs v1). `SpikeSortingPipelineParameters` lives in `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v0/spikesorting_populator.py:40` (confirmed by grep — only v0 has it; `__init__.py:35,82` re-exports it). It does NOT exist in v1 at all. `ls src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` shows no populator file, and `grep -rn "pipeline_parameters_name|PipelineParameters|preset" src/spyglass/spikesorting/v1/` returns nothing. The task is to find v1<->v2 divergences; a v0 feature that v1 itself already dropped is not a v1<->v2 divergence.

2. Rationale IS documented (refutes "no docstring states it"). The v2 module docstring at `src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/pipeline.py:1-20` explicitly states: "The orchestrator focuses on the minimum-viable single-session path; richer surfaces (metrics + auto-curation, concat sorts, cross-session matching, UI hooks) come in later versions." This directly explains why the 4-field _Preset omits waveform/metric/auto-curation — those stages aren't in scope for the v2 single-session orchestrator yet. The `_Preset` class docstring (lines 29-38) further clarifies that "``params`` Lookup row names must exist in the database before ``run_v2_pipeline`` is called; the preset itself does NOT insert Lookup rows" — explaining the Pydantic-vs-Manual-table design choice.

3. v2 IS tested (refutes implied missing coverage). `tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py` has `test_run_v2_pipeline_end_to_end_and_idempotent` (line 2286), `test_run_v2_pipeline_clusterless_preset` (line 3297), and `test_run_v2_pipeline_clusterless_default_handles_zero_units_gracefully` (line 3408) which exercise `run_v2_pipeline` and the _PRESETS dict.

4. Line numbers and structural facts are accurate (v0 table at 40-73 with 7 fields; v2 _Preset at 29-44 with 4 fields; _PRESETS dict at 64-83) — but the framing as a v1<->v2 divergence is wrong, and the "no documented rationale" premise is contradicted by the file's own module docstring.

Verdict: refuted. The reviewer compared v2 against v0, missed the v2 docstring rationale, and missed three pipeline tests. If reframed correctly as "v0 had a Manual-table preset bundle; v2's orchestrator-level analog is a code-versioned dict that intentionally omits stages deferred to later versions," this becomes a benign intentional_justified scope decision, not unjustified drift.

untested_branches.description: Recording.get_recording rebuild path when the cached AnalysisNwbfile is missing on disk: `if not Path(abs_path).exists(): self._rebuild_nwb_artifact(key)`
untested_branches.why_risky: No test deletes the on-disk artifact and re-fetches via get_recording. A regression here (silent rebuild path becoming a hard error, or recomputing into the wrong slot) would only surface in production when a user moved/lost an analysis NWB. The rebuild also re-runs the full preprocessing pipeline, which silently masks data divergence if the raw NWB has changed.

untested_branches.description: The entire _rebuild_nwb_artifact method and its hash-mismatch warning branch (`if rebuilt_hash != row['cache_hash']`) have no direct test coverage. No test triggers a hash drift to verify the user-facing logger.warning fires with the right content.
untested_branches.why_risky: Hash mismatch is the primary integrity signal for upstream raw-NWB drift or SI version skew. If the comparison silently inverts or the message format changes, users would not see the warning and would silently consume drifted data. Also: the row is intentionally NOT auto-deleted, so a regression that DOES delete it would silently destroy curation lineage.

untested_branches.description: Invalid sort_reference_electrode_id branch in _apply_pre_motion_preprocessing: `elif ref_channel_id != -1: raise ValueError(...)`. Only -1 (skip), -2 (global), and positive ids are exercised; values like -3 or -5 are never tested.
untested_branches.why_risky: SortGroupV2 stores this as a plain int with no schema validation, so a user/automation could plausibly write -3 or -10 (e.g. a typo on a negative sentinel). The guard exists precisely because the chain of elifs silently falls through with no preprocessing applied for any negative value other than -2 -- but no test confirms the guard actually fires.

untested_branches.description: set_group_by_shank `if not proposed: raise ValueError(...)` branch when every shank is filtered out (e.g. all unitrode + ref-electrode-group skip combination).
untested_branches.why_risky: Tests cover unitrode-skip (line 8267) returning a non-empty result, but none drives the filter all the way to zero proposed groups. A regression that changes the filter ordering (e.g. checking omit_unitrode after omit_ref) could silently produce empty group inserts before the guard catches them.

untested_branches.description: set_group_by_shank length-mismatch validation: `elif len(sort_group_ids) != len(proposed): raise ValueError(...)`. Same check at line 491-496 in set_group_by_electrode_table_column.
untested_branches.why_risky: Grep finds no test passing explicit sort_group_ids with wrong length. This is a user-supplied parameter and the off-by-one is a natural mistake; without coverage the message wording or the trigger condition could regress silently.

untested_branches.description: The omit_ref_electrode_group=True path that skips the electrode group containing the reference electrode. No test sets omit_ref_electrode_group=True, so the `if ref_match.any():` + `if str(ref_group) == str(e_group):` nested logic, including the skipped-list entry with `reason: reference_electrode_group`, is unexercised.
untested_branches.why_risky: The string-comparison `str(ref_group) == str(e_group)` is fragile (electrode_group_name can be numpy string vs Python str). A regression that changes the comparison semantics or fails to populate the skipped entry would silently include the ref-group when the user explicitly asked to omit it.

untested_branches.description: set_group_by_electrode_table_column empty-match ValueError: `if len(group_elecs) == 0: raise ValueError(...)` when a user-supplied values sublist matches zero electrodes.
untested_branches.why_risky: Common user error (typo in shank numbering, off-by-one column values). No test verifies the error fires with the expected message. The 8267 test only covers the unitrode skip case where len==1.

untested_branches.description: Two-branch logic mapping Spyglass electrode_ids to SpikeInterface channel ids based on whether the raw NWB carries a `channel_name` column. The test fixtures (MEArec) do not include channel_name, so only the integer-fallback path (line 1511) is exercised; the `channel_names[int(c)]` path (line 1513) is dead in tests.
untested_branches.why_risky: Production Frank-lab NWBs DO carry channel_name; v1 parity at v1/recording.py:683-712 depends on this branch resolving correctly. A regression (e.g. dropping the int() cast, or returning a numpy scalar that SI silently mishandles) would only surface on real lab data, never on the test fixtures.

untested_branches.description: Branch picking timestamps_override vs recording.get_times() based on n_selected_intervals. The single-interval persisted-override path is covered by test_recording_single_interval_persists_repaired_timestamps; the multi-interval `else: saved_times = _get_recording_timestamps(recording)` branch (line 1302) has no test that simultaneously triggers the repair AND the multi-interval concat path.
untested_branches.why_risky: If the multi-interval saved-times derivation accidentally used the override (gap-spanning envelope), the truncation guard at make_insert would spuriously fire on every disjoint sort. Conversely a regression here could silently store wrong duration_s on disjoint-interval rows.

untested_branches.description: The `existing_analysis_file_name is None and analysis_file_name is not None` cleanup branch that unlinks the staged file when a post-write step (e.g. _get_recording_timestamps) raises. Cleanup-on-insert-failure (line 1091-1100) is tested; cleanup-on-compute-failure here is not.
untested_branches.why_risky: If gain extraction or timestamp resolution raises after _write_nwb_artifact succeeds, this branch is the only thing preventing an orphan AnalysisNwbfile. A regression (swapping the `is None` to `is not None`) would silently leak files OR delete the canonical rebuild cache.

untested_branches.description: Branch raising `DuplicateSelectionError` when `len(existing) > 1` for a logical identity. test_single_session_pipeline.py:5384 tests DuplicateSelectionError but only against SortingSelection, not against RecordingSelection.insert_selection specifically.
untested_branches.why_risky: This guard is the only thing that catches direct dj.insert duplicates bypassing the helper. The error wording calls it 'an integrity bug', meaning the right detection path matters for forensics. No test bypasses the helper to verify the trap fires for RecordingSelection.

untested_branches.description: The branch that allows additive insert when `explicit_sort_group_ids and not overlap`. test_set_group_by_shank_refuses_overlapping_rerun (line 80) covers the refusal path; no test exercises a successful additive insert by passing explicit non-overlapping sort_group_ids to set_group_by_shank against an already-populated session.
untested_branches.why_risky: This is the documented escape hatch for rerunning the constructor without delete_existing_entries. A regression here would silently force users into the destructive overwrite path, exactly the v1 regression the gate was designed to fix.

untested_branches.description: The 4-condition gate (`len(unique_probes)==1 and probe=='tetrode_12.5' and len(channel_ids)==4 and len(unique_groups)==1`) decides whether the legacy tetrode patch applies. Only the all-true path is exercised by the tetrode_60s fixture; the false-by-any-of-4-reasons fall-through has no negative test (e.g. tetrode with 3 channels after a bad-channel drop, or mixed probe types).
untested_branches.why_risky: Silent geometry attachment on a non-tetrode-12.5 group would feed Kilosort/MS5 the wrong contact positions, producing plausible but wrong sort output. The condition is brittle (string compare 'tetrode_12.5'; len==4 hardcoded) and a regression flipping any inequality would never trip a test.

untested_branches.description: ALL non-stub branches in session_group.py are untested: SessionGroup.create_group (raises NotImplementedError), SessionGroup.is_multi_day (raises NotImplementedError), MotionCorrectionParameters.insert1 (Pydantic-validates row['params']), MotionCorrectionParameters.insert_default, ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection (NotImplementedError), ConcatenatedRecording.make (NotImplementedError). No test in tests/spikesorting/v2/ imports from spyglass.spikesorting.v2.session_group at all.
untested_branches.why_risky: MotionCorrectionParameters.insert1 (line 139-144) WILL be called in production once the concat consumer lands -- its Pydantic validation is the only thing preventing bad blobs from being persisted, and _DEFAULT_CONTENTS at module-import time invokes MotionCorrectionParamsSchema(preset=...).model_dump() three times. A regression in the schema or in insert1 would surface only when a user inserts a custom preset, with no test catching it. The NotImplementedError sentinels are also not tested -- a typo silently swallowing the exception would let users believe a no-op succeeded.

untested_branches.description: Sort-key tolerance for non-numeric electrode_group_name strings. test_electrode_group_sort_key directly tests the helper at test_single_session_pipeline.py:2081-2102, but no end-to-end test runs set_group_by_shank against an NWB with non-decimal electrode_group_name (e.g. 'probeA').
untested_branches.why_risky: The unit test catches a regression in the key itself, but a regression in how set_group_by_shank passes the key (e.g. forgetting `key=_electrode_group_sort_key` on line 289) would silently revert to `int()`-fails on the first non-numeric group name. No integration test exercises this on a real NWB shape.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactSelection.insert_selection raises ValueError when 'artifact_params_name' is absent from key (only one source key given, but no params name).
untested_branches.why_risky: A caller that passes recording_id but forgets artifact_params_name gets this targeted error instead of an opaque KeyError. The branch was never grep-matched in tests/spikesorting/v2/ — the 'exactly one source key' test only validates the source-key XOR (lines 633-655), not the master-field check. If the docstring identity of this branch silently changed (e.g., to a KeyError) downstream notebooks would lose the helpful diagnostic.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactSelection.insert_selection raises DuplicateSelectionError when the join surfaces multiple master rows for the same logical identity (the 'len(unique) > 1' branch).
untested_branches.why_risky: The DuplicateSelectionError test at test_single_session_pipeline.py:5384 covers RecordingSelection, NOT ArtifactSelection. If a raw dj.insert1 (bypassing the helper) plants duplicate master rows + source parts, ArtifactSelection.insert_selection should raise; instead a silent regression to 'return first match' would let downstream Sorting/Curation consumers index a non-deterministic master, producing different artifact_ids per call.

untested_branches.description: _ensure_lookup_row_exists branch: when ArtifactDetectionParameters has no row matching artifact_params_name, insert_selection raises the clear 'run insert_default()' ValueError instead of an opaque FK IntegrityError.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test grep-matches '_ensure_lookup_row_exists' for ArtifactSelection nor 'required Lookup row not found' / 'insert_default()' messages tied to ArtifactDetectionParameters. Every test calls ArtifactDetectionParameters.insert_default() in setup before insert_selection, so this user-facing diagnostic is dead code from the test suite's perspective. If a refactor removes the pre-check, fresh installs hit cryptic 'foreign key constraint fails' errors.

untested_branches.description: SharedArtifactGroup.insert_group raises ValueError when members span more than one session ('members span N sessions').
untested_branches.why_risky: No test creates members across two nwb_file_name sessions (the smoke fixture is single-session, and test_shared_artifact_group_insert_rejects_mismatched_durations / _dtypes both reuse the same nwb_file_name). This invariant is the foundational guarantee that make_compute's aggregate_channels won't crash on incompatible time axes — the per-frame z-score and proportion-above-threshold arithmetic in _detect_artifacts depends on it. If the check silently breaks, populate fails deep inside SI with an opaque shape mismatch.

untested_branches.description: SharedArtifactGroup.insert_group raises ValueError when members have differing sampling_frequency values.
untested_branches.why_risky: The duration/n_samples mismatch (line 328-337) and dtype mismatch (line 339-344) branches each have dedicated tests, but no test surfaces members with distinct sampling_frequency. A drift here would let SI.aggregate_channels crash at populate time with an opaque error — exactly the failure mode this guard was designed to prevent (the same pattern as the documented n_samples / dtype checks).

untested_branches.description: SharedArtifactGroup.insert_group wraps Recording.get_recording() failures in a RuntimeError with diagnostic context ('failed to load preprocessed recording for recording_id=...').
untested_branches.why_risky: No test exercises this except block. If a recording_id passes the (Recording & {...}) existence check at lines 233-243 but get_recording() raises (e.g., the cached preprocessed NWB on disk has been moved/deleted), the user should get the diagnostic-wrapped RuntimeError. Without coverage, a refactor that swallows or strips the wrapper would re-introduce opaque pickling/NWB errors at insert time.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactDetection.make_fetch raises RuntimeError when SharedArtifactGroup has zero members ('insert_group should have rejected the empty case').
untested_branches.why_risky: The check is a defense-in-depth re-verification of an invariant that insert_group already enforces. No test plants an empty SharedArtifactGroup (via super_delete on Member rows after the master is created) and then calls populate. If insert_group's empty-list check regressed and this guard also regressed silently, ArtifactDetection.make would silently process a 0-channel union or fail later in SI.aggregate_channels.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactDetection.make_fetch raises RuntimeError on an unexpected source.kind ('unexpected source kind ...').
untested_branches.why_risky: Catch-all guard — only fires if SourceResolution.kind grows a new variant that make_fetch is not aware of. No test mocks resolve_source to return an unknown kind. If a future contributor adds a third source part (e.g., probe-level shared artifact) and updates resolve_source but forgets the make_fetch dispatch, the failure would only surface at populate time on a real recording.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactDetection.make_compute raises RuntimeError when source.kind=='shared_artifact_group' but member_recording_ids is falsy ('make_fetch contract violated').
untested_branches.why_risky: Contract-violation guard against make_fetch returning bad shape. No test calls make_compute directly with the shared-group kind and member_recording_ids=None. Because the integration test (test_shared_artifact_group_populate_end_to_end) always exercises the happy path via populate, a regression that decoupled the two methods could silently feed empty member_recording_ids to an si.aggregate_channels call on an empty list.

untested_branches.description: _detect_artifacts returns _np.empty((0, 2)) when the sliver-filter (min_length_s threshold) removes every kept interval.
untested_branches.why_risky: OR-mode test at test_single_session_pipeline.py:4250 produces shape (0, 2) by flagging the entire recording (so 'kept' itself is empty BEFORE the sliver filter). No test takes a non-empty kept list and watches the min_length_s filter wipe all entries (e.g., min_length_s greater than every gap between detected artifact runs). Downstream consumers (Sorting._apply_artifact_mask) may not handle a (0, 2) valid_times produced via the sliver path identically to one produced via 'everything flagged' — silent regressions in either path would surface as empty sorts.

untested_branches.description: get_artifact_removed_intervals raises ValueError when key lacks 'artifact_id'.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test passes a key without artifact_id; the check sits below resolve_source which would also raise (SchemaBypassError) on an empty key. If a caller passes only a partial restriction (e.g., {'shared_artifact_group_name': X}) expecting bulk fetch semantics, the diagnostic error message guides them; without test coverage, a refactor that re-orders the call site could regress to a confusing fetch1 error.

untested_branches.description: ArtifactDetection.delete IntervalList cleanup: 'if len(rows) == 0: continue' branch skips already-missing IntervalList rows, then delegates to rows.delete(safemode=False).
untested_branches.why_risky: Tests cover the happy path (rows present, deleted) and the SchemaBypassError-orphan path (logged-and-skipped). No test exercises the case where resolve_source succeeded but the corresponding IntervalList row was already removed by a separate cleanup script (race). A regression that called rows.delete() on an empty restriction would attempt to cautious_delete the entire IntervalList table.

untested_branches.description: _detect_artifacts amplitude_thresh_uV is None branch ('above_amp = zeros'), exercised only when zscore_thresh is set alone.
untested_branches.why_risky: test_detect_artifacts_zscore_only (line 4128) does exercise this path on synthetic data, BUT the combined-mode invariant the code comment depends on ('zeros | above_x == above_x') is not regression-tested via a direct comparison between (amplitude=None, zscore=X) and (amplitude=X, zscore=None) producing matching outputs on a single-channel-amplitude trace. A silent regression to AND-combine semantics inside _detect_artifacts would slip through the OR test (line 4250) because that test only asserts shape (0,2).

untested_branches.description: delete() safemode dispatch: passes through to super().delete with kwargs preserved when safemode is None vs explicit safemode.
untested_branches.why_risky: Tests cover delete() (line 954, implicit safemode=None) and delete(safemode=False) (lines 5447, 6384, 8329) but NOT delete(safemode=True). Spyglass's cautious_delete reads safemode from dj.config; a regression that always set safemode=False would silently strip the interactive confirmation that safemode=True provides for shared-lab sessions.

untested_branches.description: prune_orphaned_selections(dry_run=False) branch where 'orphans' is empty: returns empty list without entering the for-loop deletion.
untested_branches.why_risky: test_prune_orphaned_selections_finds_and_cleans (line 1706) injects an orphan first, so the empty-orphans path is not directly tested. A regression that mistakenly cautious_delete'd the master under empty restriction would wipe ArtifactSelection. The 'or not orphans' short-circuit on line 531 is what prevents that.

untested_branches.description: Sorting.get_unit_brain_regions concat-source dispatch: when source.kind == 'concatenated_recording' and allow_anchor_member=False, it raises ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError; with allow_anchor_member=True it sets resolution='anchor_member' and dispatches to unit_brain_region_df.
untested_branches.why_risky: Neither the error path nor the anchor_member resolution label is exercised in tests (grep finds zero references to ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError, allow_anchor_member, or 'anchor_member' in tests/spikesorting/v2/). For concat-backed sorts this is the entire brain-region accessor; a silent rename or label drift (e.g. resolution string typo, missing kwarg) would not surface until a concat-pipeline notebook breaks. Since the concat schema is declared 'final under the zero-migration policy', wrong labels could lock in.

untested_branches.description: _populate_unit_part raises NonIntegerUnitIDError when a sorter emits a unit_id that does not convert to int.
untested_branches.why_risky: Zero test coverage (grep finds no NonIntegerUnitIDError reference in v2 tests). The contract is exception name + helpful message; a future refactor that swaps in a plain TypeError, drops the helpful message, or silently coerces a non-int via int() of a float NaN (raises ValueError that does fall through, but message would not match) would not be caught. Important because external sorters routinely emit string IDs.

untested_branches.description: _populate_unit_part raises RuntimeError 'peak channel ... not in sort group ...' when the analyzer's extremum channel id is not in the sort group's electrode_by_id map.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test exercises this path (grep finds no reference to 'peak channel' or the RuntimeError message). This is the only guard against a sort-group/recording channel-id mismatch silently producing rows pointing to wrong electrodes. If the dict-lookup ever changes (e.g. peak_chan typed as numpy int and lookup keys typed as Python int, or off-by-one channel re-id after probe remap), it could either crash unexpectedly or stop raising on real mismatches. Wrong-electrode unit rows corrupt downstream brain-region joins.

untested_branches.description: Full rebuild path: triggered by Sorting.get_analyzer when the analyzer folder is missing on disk. Includes a concat-source NotImplementedError, an artifact-mask re-application using a re-fetched IntervalList valid_times, and a try/except that rmtrees a partial folder on rebuild failure.
untested_branches.why_risky: No tests exercise _rebuild_analyzer_folder (grep finds zero matches). Three independent branches lack coverage: (1) the missing-folder trigger inside get_analyzer (line 851-852), (2) the artifact-mask re-application path (line 878-901) which re-issues DB I/O that bypasses make_fetch's pre-fetched obs_intervals, and (3) the partial-folder cleanup on rebuild failure (913-922). A silent change to _analyzer_path semantics or to load_sorting_analyzer error behavior could turn the rebuild path into either a permanent KeyError or a silent stale-folder load.

untested_branches.description: get_sorting returns an empty NumpySorting for zero-unit sorts, AND has an unit_ids = [int(uid) for uid in si_sorting.unit_ids] cast that the docstring says 'would raise on string-typed unit IDs'.
untested_branches.why_risky: The zero-unit empty-NumpySorting path IS covered at line 3518. However the as_dataframe=True path on a zero-unit sort is not (the test only checks .get_num_units()==0). More importantly, the as_dataframe=True path on a NON-empty sort is also not covered: grep finds zero calls of Sorting().get_sorting(..., as_dataframe=True); the only as_dataframe test (test_v1_parity.py:533) just inspects the signature. The pandas DataFrame construction (line 770-796) including the int(uid) cast, the spike_times list comprehension, and the unit_id-indexed DataFrame shape are all unverified behaviorally. A regression that returns rows positionally indexed (the bug the comment warns against) would not be caught.

untested_branches.description: When _write_units_nwb raises AFTER _build_analyzer succeeded, the except block rmtrees the analyzer folder before re-raising.
untested_branches.why_risky: The existing rollback test (line 3534-3679) covers Mode B (failure inside make_insert's transaction) by patching _populate_unit_part. It does not cover Mode A: an exception thrown specifically from _write_units_nwb after _build_analyzer wrote a folder. A 5-50 GB analyzer-folder leak per failed NWB write is precisely the failure mode the cleanup guards against. The inner except for cleanup_exc at line 621 is also untested and marked pragma: no cover.

untested_branches.description: insert_selection raises ValueError on zero/both source keys, and NotImplementedError when concat_recording_id is supplied.
untested_branches.why_risky: The both-sources/zero-sources ValueError IS covered (test at line 635-648). The concat NotImplementedError IS covered (test at line 5081). However the message 'recording_id={set/unset}' formatting is unverified for the both-sources case (the test passes neither; there is no test that passes both). A user passing both keys would get the ValueError but the helpful 'both supplied' diagnostic could silently drift.

untested_branches.description: insert_selection raises ValueError when 'sorter' or 'sorter_params_name' is missing from the key.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test covers the missing-sorter or missing-sorter_params_name branch (grep finds no test matching 'requires.*sorter' for this path). Users who forget either key currently get a clear error; a refactor that defers the check to the FK insertion would produce a confusing IntegrityError. Low impact because the error path is simple, but it is the only contract-enforcement before the FK insert.

untested_branches.description: Before creating a new master row, insert_selection calls _ensure_lookup_row_exists to translate a would-be FK IntegrityError into a clear 'missing default row' message.
untested_branches.why_risky: Zero coverage (grep finds no test for the missing-default-row path in v2/sorting.py). A user who skips SorterParameters.insert_default() and immediately calls insert_selection should get the helpful error rather than a raw IntegrityError. Refactors to _ensure_lookup_row_exists semantics (return-vs-raise, message format) could regress silently.

untested_branches.description: When sorter == 'mountainsort4' and numpy lacks the 'Inf' attribute, _np.Inf = _np.inf is assigned to work around a removed numpy 2.0 alias used by spikeextractors 0.9.11.
untested_branches.why_risky: Zero test coverage (grep finds no references to np.Inf or numpy.Inf in v2 tests). The branch is gated on (a) sorter='mountainsort4', and (b) numpy>=2.0 (the only build where hasattr returns False). If MS4 is dropped from the install matrix, this branch silently becomes dead code; if numpy < 2.0 is the test environment, the workaround is never exercised. A regression in the workaround (wrong attribute name, conditional inversion) only fails on a production NWB sort.

untested_branches.description: When sorter.lower() in _MATLAB_SORTERS (kilosort2_5, kilosort3, ironclust), run_kwargs['singularity_image']=True is set AND a strip-kwargs filter removes tempdir/mp_context/max_threads_per_process from sorter_params.
untested_branches.why_risky: Zero test coverage (grep finds no test references to _MATLAB_SORTERS, kilosort2_5, kilosort3, ironclust, or singularity_image in v2 tests). If a user inserts a custom SorterParameters row for one of these MATLAB sorters and one of the stripped kwargs is silently dropped (or, conversely, a new kwarg should be stripped but isn't), the sort either fails inside the container or runs with wrong params. The docstring explicitly says users 'can extend this set by subclassing' but the extension mechanism is not tested either.

untested_branches.description: _apply_artifact_mask returns the unmodified recording when frame_ranges is empty (no artifact gaps fall within the recording window).
