untested_branches.why_risky: The general artifact-mask test (line 2905-2998) constructs a single carved-out gap; it does not exercise the empty-frame-ranges case where valid_times completely cover the recording (so no artifact frames remain). This is the 'no-op' code path and a regression that erroneously feeds an empty np.concatenate to sip.remove_artifacts could silently raise or alter the signal. Important because the 'none' artifact preset writes exactly this shape (single all-valid interval), and Sorting routinely runs against it.

untested_branches.description: When noise_levels is None in params, the key is removed and random_slices_kwargs={'seed': _random_seed} is installed; _random_seed comes from job_kwargs.get('random_seed', 0).
untested_branches.why_risky: The strip-random_seed test at line 4042 covers noise_levels=[1.0] explicit path (line 4069). The auto-MAD path (noise_levels=None) where the explicit seed pin is installed is not directly verified to write the pinned seed into random_slices_kwargs. A regression that drops the seed pin (or pins to None instead of 0) would silently reintroduce the non-determinism that PR #3359 introduced -- the very bug this code exists to neutralize. The shipped smoke/synthetic-fixture rows omit noise_levels, so this branch IS what production hits, yet it is the less-tested of the two.

untested_branches.description: When noise_levels is supplied as a singleton (e.g. [1.0]), it is broadcast via np.full to length recording.get_num_channels() because SI's locally_exclusive indexes noise_levels[chan] per channel.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test directly verifies the broadcast (grep finds no test referencing nl.size == 1, np.full, or broadcast-to-n_channels in tests). The detect-peaks test at line 4069 supplies noise_levels=[1.0] but only checks the random_seed stripping, not whether the array reaches SI as length n_channels. A change that drops the broadcast (e.g. asarray without the size==1 check) would crash SI mid-detection only on real multi-channel recordings, not on a single-channel smoke test.

untested_branches.description: delete() collects analyzer_folder paths BEFORE the cascade delete, then passes safemode-or-defaults through super().delete(), then rmtrees the collected paths. Includes a safemode is None vs not-None branch.
untested_branches.why_risky: The existing test at line 6059-6099 invokes delete(safemode=False) only. The safemode=None branch (line 952-953) where safemode is omitted -- the default path -- is not tested. The folders_to_remove pre-fetch is also unverified for empty restrictions (delete on empty set). The post-cascade rmtree must check folder.exists() because the analyzer may have been removed elsewhere; the False branch (folder already gone) is uncovered.

untested_branches.description: ValueError raised when sorting_id has no rows in Sorting (both Sorting.Unit AND Sorting are empty). Tests grep negative for the string 'Populate Sorting first' or any insert_curation call with a non-existent sorting_id.
untested_branches.why_risky: If a caller passes a bogus sorting_id, this guard is supposed to fail loudly with a clear instruction. A regression that breaks the message (or the check entirely) would either crash deeper in `_build_curated_unit_rows`/`_stage_curated_units_nwb` with a less helpful error, or silently produce a malformed curation. Tests should pass `sorting_key={'sorting_id': <missing-uuid>}` and assert this specific ValueError.

untested_branches.description: Invalid `metrics_source` value triggers the `except ValueError` from `MetricsSource(metrics_source)` and is re-raised with a guidance message. Search for 'MetricsSource' and 'metrics_source' in tests/spikesorting/v2/ shows only the default `'manual'` is ever used (one assertion at line 1847 of test_single_session_pipeline.py). No test passes 'analyzer_curation', 'figpack', or an invalid string.
untested_branches.why_risky: Three of the four documented MetricsSource enum values (`analyzer_curation`, `figpack`, plus the typo path) have ZERO behavioral coverage. A regression in the enum coercion (e.g., import failure, accepting bad strings) would only surface when downstream code (figpack_curation, metric_curation stubs) eventually integrates. Schema-first defaults were the cause of the prior 1400x divergence bug; this is the same shape of risk.

untested_branches.description: Idempotent-root WARN-AND-RETURN path: when a root curation already exists AND the caller passes NO non-default args (labels/merge_groups/description all falsy), the function logs a warning and returns existing_root[0] without inserting. The companion error path (line 285-296, raises when caller passed args) is tested by test_curation_insert_idempotent_rejects_new_args (line 8376). The all-defaults silent-return branch is referenced in a docstring at line 1891 as `test_root_curation_idempotent` but that test does not exist.
untested_branches.why_risky: A regression could swap the condition (e.g., always raise on repeat, or always return) and silently break user workflows that repeatedly call insert_curation(sorting_key=sk) as a guard. The dangling docstring reference suggests the test was renamed or removed; the branch is now uncovered. Severity bumped because the comment claims this is parity with `v1/curation.py:88-93`.

untested_branches.description: _validate_labels rejects when `labels[unit_id]` is not a list/tuple (e.g., a bare string like `{0: 'mua'}` instead of `{0: ['mua']}`). Greps for 'list of labels', 'must be a list', and label-value-typing all return no test hits.
untested_branches.why_risky: Easy user mistake: callers from a v1 workflow often pass `labels={uid: 'mua'}` (scalar). Without this guard a regression would let the bare string into `_build_curated_unit_rows`/NWB write and then crash deep with an unhelpful 'CurationLabel value' or iterator error. Defense-in-depth principle: this validation is the only barrier.

untested_branches.description: Across-group overlap validation raises when the same unit appears in two merge groups (e.g., merge_groups=[[0,1],[1,2]]). test_curation_rejects_invalid_merge_groups (line 4924) exercises the shape/duplicate-member/missing-id paths but NOT the across-group overlap path. No grep hit for 'overlap' or 'belong to at most one merge group'.
untested_branches.why_risky: Across-group overlap is silently corrupting: without the guard, contributor 1 would be absorbed into TWO merged units, double-counting spikes and ambiguating the kept-unit choice. This is a true data-integrity guard; a regression would not crash but produce subtly wrong curated outputs. Pattern matches the prior 'mutable defaults silently diverge' class of v2 bugs.

untested_branches.description: Three separate `NotImplementedError` raises for concat-source sortings: in `get_recording` (931), `_stage_curated_units_nwb` (754), and `get_sort_group_info` (1218). The only concat-source test (test_sorting_selection_rejects_concat_source at line 5082) exercises the SortingSelection layer NOT these three CurationV2 surfaces. No grep hit for 'concat-source sorts are not yet supported' or analogous CurationV2-side concat assertions.
untested_branches.why_risky: If a future commit wires up concat sortings through SortingSelection without these three guards firing, CurationV2 would silently try to fetch a non-existent `recording_id` and either crash with a confusing fetch1 error or produce a malformed curation. The three guards are the only line of defense at the CurationV2 surface; one wrong edit removes correctness without breaking any test.

untested_branches.description: Two early-return branches in `get_merged_sorting`: (a) line 1088-1089 returns base verbatim when `merges_applied=True`; (b) line 1096-1097 returns base when no merge group has >1 contributor. The only `get_merged_sorting` test (line 4784) exercises the apply_merge=False + non-trivial merge path. No test calls `get_merged_sorting` on an `apply_merge=True` curation or on one with no real merge groups.
untested_branches.why_risky: If branch (a) regresses (e.g., the bool() check inverts), v2 would re-apply MergeUnitsSorting over already-merged units, asking SI to merge contributors that no longer exist in the base sorting -- raising an opaque SI error. If branch (b) regresses, every unmerged curation would unnecessarily wrap MergeUnitsSorting with empty units_to_merge, possibly accepted by SI but a perf regression. Both are silent: same correctness shape as the 1400x defaults bug.

untested_branches.description: When `include_labels` is supplied, restricts `unit_restriction` to units carrying at least one of the listed labels by joining UnitLabel. Grep for 'include_labels' across tests returns zero hits. The only get_unit_brain_regions coverage (test_curation_v2_auto_registers_in_merge_table at line 2022) goes through the merge dispatcher with the default include_labels=None.
untested_branches.why_risky: Documented feature gated by a parameter with NO behavioral test. A regression in the join logic (e.g., changing AND vs OR semantics, missing units that carry an excluded plus included label) would silently change which units a paper attributes to which regions. This is the kind of indexing/masking change the scientific-code-change-audit warns about: plausible-looking numbers can be wrong.

untested_branches.description: Concat-source guard: raises ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError unless `allow_anchor_member=True`, in which case it sets resolution='anchor_member'. Greps for 'allow_anchor_member' and 'ConcatBrainRegionAmbiguousError' across tests/spikesorting/v2/ return zero hits.
untested_branches.why_risky: Both branches (the raise AND the anchor-member opt-in) are uncovered. The error path is supposed to prevent silently wrong brain-region assignments on concat-backed sortings; the opt-in is the documented escape hatch. A regression that swaps the conditional or drops the exception would let users silently produce wrong per-unit region assignments on concat sorts -- the exact correctness failure mode the exception was added to prevent.

untested_branches.description: The `if any(all_labels)` branch -- add the indexed curation_label column ONLY when at least one unit has non-empty labels. The 'any labels present' branch is covered by test_curation_label_post_curation_is_indexed_ragged_list (line 6004). The companion 'all-empty labels' branch (skip add_unit_column entirely) is implicitly exercised when labels={} but no test asserts the column is ABSENT in that case; an external reader doing `nwb_sorting.get('curation_label', [])` (cited in the code comment at line 826-828) depends on the column being missing, not empty.
untested_branches.why_risky: If a regression changes the condition to `if all_labels:` (always true since `all_labels` is a non-empty list-of-lists), pynwb would try to write an all-empty ragged column and either fail dtype inference (loud crash) or write an empty column that misparses on read. No test pins the 'absent column on no-labels' invariant.

untested_branches.description: Two `if X: insert(X)` guards: skip `cls.UnitLabel.insert(unit_label_rows)` when empty (line 466-467) and skip `cls.MergeGroup.insert(merge_group_rows)` when empty (line 468-469). The merge_group_rows path always has at least the self-entries (lines 670-679 ensure every Unit has >=1 row), so the empty-guard for MergeGroup only fires when there are zero units AND zero merge groups -- which is the empty-curation case tested at line 3243. No test specifically calls labels={} on a sorting with units to assert no UnitLabel rows are inserted (only happy path through curation_id=0 fetches at line 1822 cover this implicitly).
untested_branches.why_risky: Lower severity than the others because the surrounding behavior is exercised. But a regression that drops the `if unit_label_rows:` guard would call `Part.insert([])` which on some DataJoint versions raises -- breaking ALL no-label curations (the default case). Worth a one-line assertion test.

untested_branches.description: When apply_merge=True the fresh id is allocated ONLY when `len(int_group) > 1`; the singleton case is rejected upstream by the >=2 validation (line 610-618). The `else: key = min(int_group)` branch on line 657 is only reachable when apply_merge=False (singleton case is already rejected). The test at line 4885 covers apply_merge=True with multi-merge-only groups; no test exercises a mix of multi-and-non-multi groups under apply_merge.
untested_branches.why_risky: The dead-looking `else` branch at line 657 is reachable in practice only via apply_merge=False. If a future commit relaxes the >=2 validation, the apply_merge=True path would silently pick min(group) for a singleton instead of the previously-rejected behavior, and the curation would still write but with a duplicate-id collision against a non-merged unit. Pattern-match: the same 'silent contributor double-counting' shape as the across-group overlap bug.

untested_branches.description: _get_recording_timestamps multi-segment branch: when recording.get_num_segments() > 1, concatenate per-segment times into one (total_frames,) array.
untested_branches.why_risky: This branch is the entire reason the helper exists (the docstring lists 'Multi-segment NWB support' as responsibility #1). All tests in test_v1_parity.py use fake recordings that return get_num_segments()=1, and no other test exercises the multi-segment path. An epoch-stitched recording would silently report just segment 0's times, corrupting timestamp alignment for all downstream artifact/sort intervals. No test would catch a regression in the cumsum_frames slicing.

untested_branches.description: _assert_v2_db_safe: all three branches (env-var override at :184-185, safe-host pass at :187-189, RuntimeError raise at :191-198) are untested.
untested_branches.why_risky: This is the LAST LINE OF DEFENSE preventing v2 schema registration from clobbering a production database. A refactor that broke the host check (typo in the frozenset, wrong dj.config key, inverted condition) or broke the override env-var read would either deadlock test imports or, much worse, silently allow non-local writes. The docstring explicitly calls this 'the last line of defense if some other code path repointed dj.config'.

untested_branches.description: _assert_schema_version_matches mismatch ValueError branch: raises when row['params_schema_version'] (outer) disagrees with row['params']['schema_version'] (inner).
untested_branches.why_risky: test_integrity.py:240-287 exercises the surrounding _validate_params plumbing but only with consistent outer+inner versions, and tests/spikesorting/v2/ contains no row insert where outer != inner. The docstring explicitly warns 'downstream code that branches on the outer column will silently route v2 rows to v1 behavior (or vice versa)'. If this guard regressed (e.g. a typo flipping the !=), bogus rows would land and the silent-routing bug it was designed to prevent would resurface — exactly the kind of cascading divergence the schema-first design exists to prevent.

untested_branches.description: transaction_or_noop already-in-transaction branch (line 29 yields bare, no nested dj transaction).
untested_branches.why_risky: All five call sites (recording.py:398, :556, :1075, etc.) wrap source-part-master inserts; an incorrect inversion (e.g. unconditionally opening connection.transaction) would crash with 'transactions cannot be nested' inside a populate cascade. No test in tests/spikesorting/v2/ asserts the nested-call behavior — tests only confirm the context manager is *used* (AST scan in test_v1_parity.py:785-868). A regression here would corrupt the source-part atomicity guarantee at populate time only.

untested_branches.description: _ensure_lookup_row_exists ValueError branch (lines 355-363): raised when a required Lookup row is missing before insert_selection.
untested_branches.why_risky: Called from artifact.py:481, sorting.py:325, recording.py:717 to convert opaque DataJoint IntegrityError ('foreign key constraint fails') into actionable error messages with 'Run X.insert_default()' guidance. No test exercises the missing-row path — all v2 tests call insert_default() first. A regression (e.g. wrong helper_name in the error string, or skipping the check) would re-surface the opaque MySQL error that this helper exists to suppress, degrading the notebook UX precisely when users have a setup bug.

untested_branches.description: franklab_tetrode_mountainsort4 preset registration: the bundle is never end-to-end populated by any test.
untested_branches.why_risky: Only the ms5 preset (test_single_session_pipeline.py:2330) and clusterless_thresholder preset (:3450) are exercised by run_v2_pipeline. The ms4 preset string is asserted to exist in list_presets() output indirectly, but no test inserts the ms4 bundle's Lookup rows (preproc_params_name + 'franklab_tetrode_hippocampus_30kHz_ms4') through run_v2_pipeline. If the preset bundle references a Lookup row name that was renamed or never installed by insert_default, the failure surfaces only on first user invocation.

untested_branches.description: _consolidate_intervals ValueError branch: 'Input array must have shape (N_Intervals, 2)' raised when intervals.shape[1] != 2.
untested_branches.why_risky: No test in tests/spikesorting/v2/ asserts the shape rejection. Indirect coverage via test_disjoint_sort_intervals_concatenated only feeds well-shaped (N, 2) inputs. A malformed valid_times blob (e.g. (N, 3) from an upstream schema drift) would bypass the guard if the check were removed, yielding wrong frame indices and silently corrupted recordings.

untested_branches.description: _consolidate_intervals defensive sort branch: when intervals are not already monotonic, argsort by start.
untested_branches.why_risky: test_disjoint_sort_intervals_concatenated passes pre-sorted disjoint chunks (test_single_session_pipeline.py:5759 builds them in order). No test feeds unsorted intervals. If the sort were broken (e.g. argsort on the wrong axis), overlapping intervals would silently merge incorrectly, producing wrong frame slices for upstream IntervalList rows that aren't pre-sorted.

untested_branches.description: MetricsSource enum members 'analyzer_curation' and 'figpack': only the 'manual' value is tested.
untested_branches.why_risky: tests/spikesorting/v2/test_single_session_pipeline.py:1847 asserts metrics_source == 'manual'; no test inserts a CurationV2 row with metrics_source='analyzer_curation' or 'figpack'. curation.py:307 calls MetricsSource(metrics_source).value, which would reject a typo, but if either enum value were renamed/deleted in a refactor, the downstream metric_curation.py and figpack_curation.py stub ports would silently lose their provenance gate. Given those two modules are still stubs (per branch context), no integration test will catch the drift either.

untested_branches.description: parse_artifact_interval_list_name: both the str-prefix-match branch and the None-return branch are completely untested, and the function has NO callers in src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/.
untested_branches.why_risky: The docstring claims it is 'the inverse' of artifact_interval_list_name and quotes a 'merge-dispatcher leave non-artifact names alone' contract — but no merge dispatcher actually invokes it. Either this is dead code that should be removed, or there is a missing call site that the v2 merge integration expects. Either way the public API is shipped untested.

untested_branches.description: get_spiking_sorting_v2_merge_ids: only its function signature is asserted (test_v1_parity.py:294-302). Neither as_dict=True nor as_dict=False return paths are executed in any test.
untested_branches.why_risky: This is the user-facing v2 parallel of v1's get_spiking_sorting_v1_merge_ids. The docstring promises 'Unknown keys raise ValueError' — but no test passes an unknown key. A regression in SpikeSortingOutput()._get_restricted_merge_ids_v2 (the delegate) would not be caught at this layer; notebook users would hit it directly.

untested_branches.description: list_presets() helper: never called by any test.
untested_branches.why_risky: Notebook-discoverable accessor that returns sorted(_PRESETS). If a future refactor accidentally returns the dict instead of sorted names, or hides a preset, no test catches it. Low severity because the docstring example is its only contract — but the docstring lists the exact three names as expected output, and a regression that drops one would silently break notebooks.

untested_branches.description: run_v2_pipeline empty-description fallback: 'description or f"run_v2_pipeline preset={preset}"' substitutes a generated description when caller passes description="".
untested_branches.why_risky: The end-to-end test (test_single_session_pipeline.py:2331) always passes description='pipeline e2e test', and the zero-units test (:3445) omits description (uses default ''). The fallback path may or may not execute depending on how CurationV2.insert_curation handles the synthesized string — no assertion ever verifies that the generated description lands in the row. A subtle truthiness bug (e.g. empty dict vs empty string semantics) would be invisible.

synthesis.executive_summary: The v2 spike sorting refactor has 130 confirmed divergences from v1, with the largest-impact concerns concentrated in `recording.py` and `artifact.py`. Three production-relevant regressions stand out: (1) per-shank `original_reference_electrode` auto-selection from the Electrode table is silently dropped in `set_group_by_shank`, so Frank-lab tetrode rigs that relied on v1's default will now write `-1` (no reference) into every sort group unless callers explicitly opt in; (2) `omit_ref_electrode_group=True` is preserved as a kwarg but becomes a silent no-op under the default `sort_reference_electrode_id=-1`; (3) artifact detection was rewritten as a fully in-memory scan with ~3× peak memory of the full traces array, with chunked execution deferred and `job_kwargs` reduced to dead weight. Several large v1 features (MetricCuration, FigURLCuration, BurstPair, RecordingRecompute) are 3-line stubs or entirely absent in v2 and need an explicit ported/deferred decision. Test coverage is strong on the happy paths but leaves 70 branches unexercised, including high-impact ones around the AnalysisNwbfile rebuild path, hash-mismatch warning, channel_name resolution on real Frank-lab NWBs, the tetrode-geometry gating predicate, and the entire `session_group.py` module. Lower-severity findings are mostly cosmetic (metadata strings, varchar widening, z-score epsilon) and can be batched. None of the confirmed divergences are silently numerically wrong on the test fixtures, but several would surface on production-scale real data only.

top_concerns.title: Per-shank original_reference_electrode auto-selection silently dropped from set_group_by_shank
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v1 default behavior on Frank-lab tetrode rigs read the per-shank reference from electrodes['original_reference_electrode'] and wrote a different sort_reference_electrode_id per sort group. v2 takes a single int kwarg (default -1) and applies it to every group, never consulting original_reference_electrode. A user porting a v1 pipeline who relied on the default will silently write 'no reference' into every preprocessed Recording — the referencing in Recording.make at line 379 will then be skipped or applied incorrectly, biasing downstream sort outcomes. The docstring only states 'Per-group references are not supported in v2; if you need them, build the rows manually' with no migration guidance.
top_concerns.recommendation: Either (a) restore the v1 default by reading original_reference_electrode when sort_reference_electrode_id is omitted, or (b) add a hard error / loud warning when the kwarg is omitted on an NWB whose electrodes table carries non-trivial per-shank reference values. Add a CHANGELOG entry and a docstring example showing the manual per-group recipe.

top_concerns.title: omit_ref_electrode_group=True is a silent no-op under default sort_reference_electrode_id=-1
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v1 looked up the per-shank reference and skipped that electrode group. v2 instead checks the passed-in sort_reference_electrode_id against the electrode table; with the -1 default, ref_match.any() is False and the omit branch silently does nothing. The kwarg is preserved at the API surface, so users believe it is taking effect. No test exercises omit_ref_electrode_group=True (grep returns zero hits across tests/spikesorting/v2/).
top_concerns.recommendation: Either raise / warn when omit_ref_electrode_group=True is passed without a positive sort_reference_electrode_id, or restore the per-shank lookup specifically for the omit branch. Add a test that fails if the flag is silently a no-op.

top_concerns.title: Artifact detection rewritten as in-memory scan; ~3× full-traces peak memory; chunked execution deferred
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v2/artifact.py:870 reads the full recording into RAM via get_traces, then materializes float32 + abs + (in z-score mode) zscores arrays for an effective ~3-4× n_samples * n_channels * 4 bytes peak. For a typical hippocampal session (1-3h × 30 kHz × 64+ ch) this is ~80+ GB. Tests use ~5000-sample synthetic recordings and never stress this. The inline comment at line 866-869 admits chunked iteration is 'follow-up work' but the v1 ChunkRecordingExecutor path is fully removed and the job_kwargs schema column is acknowledged dead weight (lines 712-721).
top_concerns.recommendation: Before this lands in production, either restore a chunked path using SI's ChunkRecordingExecutor (still available in SI 0.104) or add an explicit n_samples/duration ceiling that raises before the get_traces call. Document the limit in the schema docstring and ArtifactDetectionParameters. Drop the dead job_kwargs column if it will not be wired up.

top_concerns.title: ConcatenatedRecording / MotionCorrection / SessionGroup helpers stubbed with NotImplementedError but schema is final-shape
top_concerns.why_it_matters: session_group.py declares the final schema for SessionGroup, MotionCorrectionParameters, ConcatenatedRecordingSelection, and ConcatenatedRecording but ConcatenatedRecording.make, ConcatenatedRecordingSelection.insert_selection, SessionGroup.create_group, and SessionGroup.is_multi_day all raise NotImplementedError. The MotionCorrectionParameters Pydantic schema runs at module import time (three preset model_dumps) but no test imports session_group at all. SortingSelection's ConcatenatedRecordingSource path is correspondingly gated with NotImplementedError in Sorting.make. A user discovering the table in the schema browser has no way to know the consumer logic is not yet implemented.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a top-of-module docstring marking the entire module as a stub-with-final-shape, add at least a smoke test that asserts NotImplementedError is raised (to catch the silent-swallow regression), and add tests for MotionCorrectionParameters.insert1 schema validation before any consumer lands. Consider hiding ConcatenatedRecording from the published table graph until the consumer ships.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 has no equivalent feature

top_concerns.title: MetricCuration, FigURLCuration, BurstPair, RecordingRecompute are 3-line stubs or entirely absent
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v1 ships ~2400 lines of production curation tooling (metric_curation.py 710 lines + figurl_curation.py 322 lines + burst_curation.py 397 lines + recompute.py 981 lines). v2 has 3-line stubs for MetricCuration and figpack_curation, no BurstPair module at all, and only an inline NwbfileHasher digest column instead of RecordingRecompute. Active Frank-lab curation workflows (`CurationV1.insert_curation` → MetricCuration → BurstPair → FigURLCuration) have no v2 equivalent. Users who try to port a v1 curation notebook to v2 will hit ImportError or empty modules.
top_concerns.recommendation: Publish a v2 roadmap that lists each stubbed feature and either (a) a target version / PR number, or (b) an explicit decision to deprecate. For MetricCuration in particular, decide whether the v2 surface will use SI's new SortingAnalyzer (in which case the legacy_si gate at v1/metric_curation.py:17 can be dropped) or keep mountainsort4 compatibility. Until then, raise an informative NotImplementedError from the stub modules with a pointer to the v1 equivalent.
top_concerns.v2_location: src/spyglass/spikesorting/v2/metric_curation.py (3 lines); v2/figpack_curation.py (3 lines); no v2/burst_curation.py; no v2/recompute.py

top_concerns.title: AnalysisNwbfile rebuild path and hash-mismatch warning are completely untested
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Recording.get_recording at line 1114-1117 transparently rebuilds a missing AnalysisNwbfile via _rebuild_nwb_artifact, which re-runs the full preprocessing pipeline. The hash-mismatch warning at line 1171-1179 is the primary integrity signal for upstream raw-NWB drift or SI version skew. No test deletes the on-disk artifact and re-fetches, and no test forces a hash drift. The row is intentionally NOT auto-deleted on hash mismatch — a regression that DID delete it would silently destroy curation lineage; conversely a regression that flipped the comparison would let users consume drifted data with no warning.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add two regression tests: (a) delete the analysis file on disk, call get_recording, assert the rebuilt file matches the original hash; (b) tamper with the raw NWB (or monkeypatch _rebuild to return a different hash), assert logger.warning fires with the expected message and the row is NOT deleted.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 had no in-band rebuild mechanism (RecordingRecompute was a separate table)

top_concerns.title: channel_name → SI channel-id resolution path is unexercised on test fixtures
top_concerns.why_it_matters: _spikeinterface_channel_ids at lines 1510-1513 picks between integer fallback and channel_names[int(c)] lookup based on whether the raw NWB carries a channel_name column. The MEArec test fixtures do not include channel_name, so only the integer-fallback path is exercised. Production Frank-lab NWBs DO carry channel_name, and v1 parity at v1/recording.py:683-712 depends on this branch resolving correctly. A regression here (dropped int() cast, numpy scalar returned) only surfaces on real lab data.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a test fixture (or monkeypatch on an existing fixture) that injects a channel_name column, and assert the resolved SI channel_ids match the expected mapping. Consider a parametrized test running both paths.

top_concerns.title: Tetrode-geometry gating predicate is a brittle 4-condition AND with no negative tests
top_concerns.why_it_matters: _maybe_apply_tetrode_geometry at lines 1681-1722 silently attaches legacy tetrode_12.5 contact positions only when (single probe) AND (probe == 'tetrode_12.5' literal) AND (4 channels exactly) AND (single electrode group). Only the all-true path is tested via tetrode_60s. A 3-channel tetrode (after bad-channel drop), a mixed-probe sort group, or a renamed probe string falls through silently — Kilosort/MS5 then receives whatever geometry SI inferred, producing plausible but wrong sort output.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add negative tests for each of the four conditions failing (3-channel tetrode, mixed probe types, renamed probe string, multi-group). When any condition fails, log at INFO level which condition failed so a user investigating sort quality can see whether the patch fired.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 did not apply this patch

top_concerns.title: SharedArtifactGroup cross-session and frequency-mismatch invariants are untested
top_concerns.why_it_matters: SharedArtifactGroup.insert_group at lines 276-284 and 290-300 enforces that members come from a single session and share a sampling_frequency. _detect_artifacts' per-frame z-score and proportion-above-threshold arithmetic depends on these invariants. Smoke fixtures are single-session and single-frequency. If either invariant silently breaks, populate fails deep inside SI with an opaque shape mismatch.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add two tests: (a) members spanning two nwb_file_names, asserting the 'members span N sessions' ValueError fires; (b) members with mismatched sampling_frequency, asserting the corresponding ValueError fires.

top_concerns.title: ArtifactSelection DuplicateSelectionError branch is unverified
top_concerns.why_it_matters: The 'len(unique) > 1' branch at lines 470-475 is the integrity trap for direct dj.insert callers bypassing the helper. The DuplicateSelectionError test at line 5384 covers RecordingSelection, not ArtifactSelection. A silent regression to 'return first match' would let downstream Sorting/Curation index a non-deterministic master and produce different artifact_ids per call.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a regression test that plants two master rows + matching parts via raw dj.insert1, then calls ArtifactSelection.insert_selection and asserts DuplicateSelectionError is raised with the 'integrity bug' wording.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 helper had different semantics

top_concerns.title: Invalid sort_reference_electrode_id values silently fall through preprocessing
top_concerns.why_it_matters: _apply_pre_motion_preprocessing at lines 1765-1770 raises ValueError for negative sentinels other than -1 and -2. SortGroupV2 stores the field as a plain int with no schema validation, so a typo like -3 or -10 is plausible. The guard exists precisely because the chain of elifs silently falls through with no preprocessing applied — but no test confirms it actually fires.
top_concerns.recommendation: Either add Pydantic-level validation on SortGroupV2 ingestion to restrict the field to {-1, -2, non-negative ints}, or add a unit test that constructs a SortGroupV2 row with sort_reference_electrode_id=-3 and asserts the guard fires with the expected message.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 used a different reference model

top_concerns.title: IntervalList row keyed by recording_id is no longer inserted
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v1 inserted a public IntervalList row at interval_list_name=recording_id (v1/recording.py:212-216, 255-257) for downstream provenance queries. v2 stores the range only on the Recording row (saved_start, saved_end, duration_s). Any external code doing IntervalList & {interval_list_name: recording_id} will silently get empty results. No internal pipeline consumer exists in either version, so the impact is on external user code only.
top_concerns.recommendation: Either (a) restore the IntervalList insert in make_insert, or (b) document the removal in the v2 module docstring and CHANGELOG and provide a one-liner recipe for users to reconstruct the IntervalList from the Recording row.

top_concerns.title: Multi-interval saved_times derivation untested under timestamp-repair path
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Lines 1297-1302 pick between timestamps_override and recording.get_times() based on n_selected_intervals. The single-interval persisted-override path has a dedicated test; the multi-interval `else: saved_times = _get_recording_timestamps(recording)` branch has no test that simultaneously triggers the repair AND the multi-interval concat path. A regression that accidentally used the override (a gap-spanning envelope) on disjoint sorts would spuriously trip the truncation guard at make_insert on every run.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a test fixture with two disjoint sort intervals AND a corrupted-timestamps repair, then assert duration_s is computed from the concat path and the truncation guard does not fire.
top_concerns.v1_location: n/a — v1 had no timestamps repair

top_concerns.title: Compute-phase cleanup branch can leak / over-delete AnalysisNwbfiles
top_concerns.why_it_matters: _compute_recording_artifact's cleanup branch at lines 1311-1323 unlinks the staged file only when existing_analysis_file_name is None AND analysis_file_name is not None AND a post-write step raises. Insert-phase cleanup (line 1091-1100) is tested; compute-phase cleanup is not. A regression flipping the `is None` check would either leak orphan files or delete the canonical rebuild cache.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a test that injects an exception from _get_recording_timestamps after a fresh _write_nwb_artifact succeeds, and assert the staged file is unlinked. Add a complementary test where existing_analysis_file_name is not None and assert the file is NOT unlinked.

top_concerns.title: v2 join_window_ms decouples from removal_window_ms but is not algebraically identical to v1
top_concerns.why_it_matters: v2 joins artifact frames whose index gap <= ceil(fs*join_window_ms*1e-3) BEFORE adding the removal-window half-padding (v2/artifact.py:944-960). v1 expands by removal_window_ms/2 then unions overlapping intervals. With both windows at 1 ms the results are approximately the same but not identical at the boundary. The 1 ms / 1 ms default makes this low-impact in practice. Tests exercise both branches of the join loop at test_single_session_pipeline.py:4260-4330.
top_concerns.recommendation: Add a one-paragraph note to ArtifactDetectionParameters' field description explaining the algorithmic difference and why a separate knob is preferred over the v1 coupling. Document in the v2 CHANGELOG / migration guide so users replaying v1 params understand the boundary case.

intentional_changes_well_justified.title: ElectricalSeries metadata strings updated to reflect new bandpass + CMR preprocessing semantics
intentional_changes_well_justified.rationale: v2 strings ('Bandpass filter + common reference', 'Pre-motion preprocessed recording', 'Sort group electrodes') at v2/recording.py:1895-1907 are arguably more accurate than v1's. No internal consumer pattern-matches on them. Confirm this matches your intent for downstream NWB metadata consumers (NWBInspector, DANDI exports). Severity: low.

intentional_changes_well_justified.title: Description varchar widened from 100 to 255 in CurationV2
intentional_changes_well_justified.rationale: v2/curation.py:63 widens the column to align with the codebase-wide varchar(255) convention (41 occurrences in src/spyglass vs only 2 of varchar(100)). The design plan at .claude/docs/plans/spikesorting-v2/designs.md:705 prescribes this. Confirm this is the intended convention for all v2 description columns. Severity: low.

intentional_changes_well_justified.title: Z-score implementation rewritten with +1e-12 std epsilon
intentional_changes_well_justified.rationale: v2/artifact.py:901-903 replaces scipy.stats.zscore with an inline computation plus 1e-12 epsilon. This eliminates the v1 NaN-when-std=0 silent-skip behavior for silent channels. Post-bandpass traces almost never have exactly-zero std so the divergence is theoretical, but v2's behavior is more defensive. Confirm you prefer the defensive zero-mean → zero behavior over v1's NaN-skip. Severity: low.

intentional_changes_well_justified.title: join_window_ms split from removal_window_ms
intentional_changes_well_justified.rationale: Decoupling the merge window from the padding window is a defensible API improvement and tests cover both branches. Confirm the boundary-case difference vs v1 (frame-index merge before padding vs time-padded union) is acceptable for replayed v1 parameter sets. Severity: low.

recommended_actions: Resolve the per-shank reference-electrode regression (top concern #1) BEFORE any Frank-lab production session is sorted with v2: either restore the v1 default by reading original_reference_electrode, or add a hard-fail guard when the kwarg is omitted on an NWB with non-trivial Electrode references. Treat as a release blocker.
Fix or loudly warn on the omit_ref_electrode_group=True silent no-op (top concern #2) in the same patch — it is the same root cause as #1.

Decide on artifact detection memory strategy (top concern #3) before users run multi-hour sessions: either reintroduce ChunkRecordingExecutor or add a duration/n_samples ceiling that raises with a clear message. Drop the dead job_kwargs schema column or wire it up; do not ship it lying about respecting parallelism.

Publish a v2 stub roadmap in the module-level docstring (top concern #5): for each of MetricCuration, FigURLCuration, BurstPair, RecordingRecompute, SessionGroup, ConcatenatedRecording, document target timeline OR explicit deprecation. Make stubs raise informative NotImplementedError pointing to the v1 fallback rather than returning empty modules.
Add a regression-test batch for the AnalysisNwbfile rebuild path and hash-mismatch warning (top concern #6) — these are the integrity signals for the entire cache layer.

Add real-data integration coverage for the channel_name resolution path and the tetrode-geometry gate (top concerns #7 and #8): inject channel_name on a fixture and add negative tests for each of the four tetrode-gate conditions.

Batch-fix the high-severity untested branches: 70 branches in total. Group as: (a) recording.py integrity branches (rebuild, hash-mismatch, channel_name, tetrode-gate, multi-interval saved_times, compute-phase cleanup, invalid ref ids); (b) artifact.py invariant branches (DuplicateSelectionError, SharedArtifactGroup cross-session/frequency, lookup-row precheck, master-field validation); (c) session_group.py NotImplementedError sentinels plus MotionCorrectionParameters.insert1 Pydantic validation. Target ~3-4 PRs grouped by file.

Batch-document low-severity intentional changes in a single migration-notes page: metadata string updates, description varchar widening, z-score epsilon, join_window_ms semantics, dropped IntervalList row. None individually warrants a release blocker; collectively they are the v1→v2 user-visible delta surface.

Add a CHANGELOG.md entry for v2 enumerating: (1) breaking API changes (single sort_reference_electrode_id, dropped per-shank lookup), (2) removed v1 features (MetricCuration, FigURLCuration, BurstPair, RecordingRecompute, recording_id-keyed IntervalList), (3) deferred features (chunked artifact, ConcatenatedRecording, cross-session unit matching), (4) metadata-string changes likely to break external pattern-matching.
After fixes land, re-run the parity audit on the patched branches to confirm the high-severity divergences are resolved and that newly-added tests cover the previously-untested branches.
